metadata.title: Japan Sake Brewery Database
metadata.description: Comprehensive database of active sake breweries in Japan, sourced from the Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association (JSS) Sakagura Search directory.
metadata.source: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association (JSS) — https://japansake.or.jp/sakagura/en/
notes: Data sourced from JSS member directory — covers JSS member breweries only
Some breweries produce shochu, awamori, or other spirits in addition to or instead of sake
Image URLs point to the JSS website CDN and may change over time
Founded year is approximate for some historical breweries
Brand names (meigara) are the sake label names, not the brewery names

breweries.name: Kunimare Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-17, Inabacho, Mashikecho, Mashike Gun, Hokkaido, 077-0204, Japan
breweries.description: Located in the northwestern part of Hokkaido, Mashike Town was once a prosperous town for its abundant herring fishery.In 1882, Kunimare Sake Brewery began when the demand for sake increased due to the booming economy caused by herring.The abundance of good water sourced from the hot and cold mountain ranges of mountains brings a wide variety of mountain and sea food.The main products of Kunimare Shuzo, which has been nurtured in a culture of hair thickening, are mainly transparent and slightly dry.Kunimare Sake Brewery have formed a council with local sake rice producers, and we are walking along with the history of Mashike, such as the development of agriculture and the culture surrounding Japan sake. Store and Reference Room Hours: 9:00-17:00, open all year round (except year-end and New Year's holidays)Tasting(free of charge) until 16:30Parking: 3 buses, 30 passenger cars
breweries.visiting_info: Store and Reference Room Hours: 9:00-17:00, open all year round (except year-end and New Year's holidays)Tasting(free of charge) until 16:30Parking: 3 buses, 30 passenger cars

breweries.name: Kobayashi Shuzou
breweries.address: 3-109, Nishiki, Kuriyamacho, Yubari Gun, Hokkaido, 069-1521, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1878 (Meiji 11), Kobayashi Shuzou has consistently pursued the question, "What kind of sake can only be brewed and enjoyed in Hokkaido?"The trademark Kitanonishiki reflects our aspiration to bring glory and distinction to this region of Hokkaido.During the era when coal mining thrived, our sake was beloved by many miners, leading to a period of increased shipments.Today, in the naturally rich land of Kuriyama Town, we remain committed to crafting sake that is deeply rooted in this area and cherished by everyone. Direct Store: "Kuramoto Kitano Nishiki Memorial Hall"Business Hours:April to October: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PMNovember to March: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PMTasting:Four varieties of limited-edition products from the brewery are always available for tasting.

breweries.name: Nihon no Koshugura
breweries.address: 1021 Yoshino, Noguchi Town, Kakogawa City, Hyogo
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Nihon no Koshugura was established in collaboration with 10 partner breweries across Japan to preserve and pass down the traditions of sake brewing and aging culture for future generations.As Japan’s first specialized aging warehouse for koshu (aged sake), we carefully curate and further mature selected aged sake from all over the country, creating new value through additional aging techniques.By utilizing wooden barrel aging and proprietary blending techniques, we offer innovative flavors that harmonize with modern culinary trends and lifestyles. The profound and complex flavors developed over many years of aging provide an unparalleled sensory experience.Through these initiatives, we have launched the ultra-long-term aged sake brand "Inishie no Bishu | INISHIE", sharing the new possibilities of aged sake with both domestic and international audiences. Business Hours:10:00 AM – 5:00 PMBusiness Days:Weekdays, excluding New Year holidays
breweries.visiting_info: Business Hours:10:00 AM – 5:00 PMBusiness Days:Weekdays, excluding New Year holidays

breweries.name: Nihon Seishu
breweries.address: 5-2, Minami 3 Johigashi, Chuo Ku, Sapporo Shi, Hokkaido, 060-0053, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1872, Chitosetsuru is the only sake brewery in Sapporo, Hokkaido.Sake rice is 90% used Hokkaido rice, using high-quality ""Kitashizuku"" and ""Ginpu"" rice produced in Shintotsukawa-cho, the largest sake rice production area in Hokkaido.The brewing water, which is important for sake brewing, is drawn from the underground water of the Toyohira River in Sapporo, and is soft water with low particulate matter and a mild taste suitable for sake brewing.In 2023, we will build a new sake brewery for the first time in 64 years, which will enable from sake making in winter and brewing in the three seasons. [Chitosekuru Sake Museum (directly-managed store)] Tasting is available from 10:00 to 18:00.Closed: Year-end and New Year holidays[Factory Tour] Weekdays 10:00-16:00, groups of about 10 people per day.For more information, please visit our website: https://nipponseishu.co.jp/chitosetsuru/brewery-tour/

breweries.address: 1-1-1, Hassamu 10 Jo, Nishi Ku, Sapporo Shi, Hokkaido, 063-0830, Japan

breweries.description: Founded in Sapporo City in 1933 as a Class A shochu manufacturer, we obtained a license to manufacture Class B shochu in 1978 and have been working on the production of Class B shochu with a focus on Hokkaido ingredients.Based on our commitment to fresh and safe ingredients in Hokkaido, we have built a factory in Assabu Town in southern Hokkaido based on the concept of ""A hometown full of joy is in Hokkaido"" and manufacture a variety of shochu, including the authentic shochu ""Kitari"".

breweries.name: Takasago Shuzou
breweries.address: 17, Miyashitadori, Asahikawa Shi, Hokkaido, 070-0030, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1899, our sake brewery is based in Asahikawa, Hokkaido. We produce and sell Pure Hokkaido Sake, crafted from the pristine spring water of the Daisetsuzan mountain range, known for its abundant flow and exceptional clarity, and high-quality sake rice grown in Hokkaido.In recent years, we have embraced eco-friendly innovations, such as the snow storage method that utilizes the unique natural environment of snowy regions. We also focus on crafting sake that highlights the distinctive characteristics of rice-growing regions within Hokkaido. Furthermore, we fully utilize approximately 50 tons of sake lees produced annually, collaborating with companies from different industries to develop innovative products.As a local sake brewery in Asahikawa, we are dedicated to creating unique offerings that can only be found here. Direct sales store: 9:00-17:00 (with free tasting), closed only during the year-end and New Year's holidaysFactory tour (free of charge) / 2 sessions at 10:00 and 15:00 (first 10 visitors each)Reservations required at least 5 days prior to the desired date.

breweries.name: Kinteki Shuzou
breweries.address: 71-7, Chuo, Shintotsukawacho, Kabato Gun, Hokkaido, 073-1103, Japan
breweries.description: Kinteki Shuzo is located in the town of Shintotsukawa, which has a rich history dating back to 1889 (Meiji 22). At that time, a catastrophic flood devastated Totsukawa Village in Nara Prefecture, leaving its residents without homes. Seeking a new beginning, the villagers migrated to Hokkaido and began cultivating the untamed wilderness.Sixteen years later, with food production stabilized, a shared desire emerged: “Let’s brew our own sake.” In 1906 (Meiji 39), the first corporate sake brewery in Hokkaido, Shintotsukawa Sake Brewing Company, was established. In 1951 (Showa 26), it was renamed Kinteki Shuzo Co., Ltd., continuing to the present day.Kinteki Shuzo focuses on using local resources—rice, water, and people—to craft its sake. The brewery takes pride in producing handmade sake in small batches, using local sake rice varieties such as Ginpu and Kitashizuku, which originated in Hokkaido and are grown in Shintotsukawa, the birthplace of Hokkaido's sake rice. Each batch is carefully and meticulously brewed, ensuring a deep connection to the region's heritage. Business Hours:8:30 AM – 5:30 PM (Monday to Friday)Store Hours:Weekdays: 8:30 AM – 5:30 PMSaturdays, Sundays, and public holidays: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PMClosed:January 1st – 3rdBrewery Tours:Available from the week after Golden Week in May to the end of SeptemberReservation required (1 week in advance)

breweries.name: Rokka Shuzou
breweries.address: 217, Toyoda, Mukaitonose, Hirosaki Shi, Aomori Ken, 036-8066, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1719 in Hirosaki City, Aomori Prefecture, our brewery celebrated its 300th anniversary in June 2023. To pursue higher-quality water and an ideal brewing environment, we relocated our brewery from the city center to the foothills of Mt. Iwaki, also known as "Tsugaru Fuji."We have launched a new brand, Torai, crafted to reflect the unique terroir of Tsugaru, using primarily sake rice grown in Aomori Prefecture. By focusing on quality through meticulous temperature control, small-batch brewing, and year-round production across three seasons, we ensure our products remain consistently fresh and of the highest quality. At the on-site direct sales shop, a tasting server is available with 6 types of sake for tasting (free of charge). You can also watch a monitor displaying the brewery's activities.Business Hours:10:00 AM – 3:00 PMClosed on:Sundays and public holidays (may change depending on the season, so please inquire in advance)Brewery tours are available for a fee and require advance reservation.

breweries.name: Morita Shoubei
breweries.address: 230, Shichinohe, Shichinohe Machi, Kamikita Gun, Aomori Ken, 039-2525, Japan
breweries.description: Tabe Takeshita Brewery took over the business from Takeshita Honten due to the lack of a successor and newly opened in June 2022. Production began in November with up-and-coming chief sake maker brewer Mr. Hamasaki.The history of the Takeshita family and the Tabe family, which took over the business, goes back to 1866, when the Takeshita family took over the brewing rights "Sakeza" from the Tabe family, one of the largest landowners in Japan, and started sake brewing.Later, the Takeshita family became a prominent family representing the region, producing the 74th Prime Minister Noboru Takeshita, and grew into a company that led the region's development. However, the business faced a crisis due to the lack of a successor, and the Takeshita family approached the Tabe family for advice, which led to the succession of the business.The concept is "fruity and outstandingly fresh sake", and the image is that of a sparkling clear sake from the summer sea in the San-in region.The first release in January 2023, Okuizumo Maemawamaya "test brew" was truly a test brew, as it was the first time for the new chief sake maker to work with the land, water, and climate. Based on last year's experience, the process was further brushed up and re-released as the "Rihachi" brand. With the up-and-coming chief sake maker Hamasaki, the Tabe family now once again took on the challenge of brewing sake. Business hours are weekdays 9:00-17:00 (closed Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays). For specific business days, please refer to the business day calendar listed on the website.Sake is sold at the brewery.If you wish to tour the brewery and sample sake, please consult with us in advance. (Please note that this service is not available at all times and may not be available due to circumstances beyond our control.)

breweries.description: This area is located in the eastern part of the Tsugaru region, this area is famous for its apples and rice, and is a land of good water, good rice, and good sake.This company is since founded in 1913, we are brewing locally brewed sake using abundant underground water from the southern Mt.Hakkouda under the company motto of ""harmonious cooperation"" and by using the best of our traditional sake brewing techniques, which we have refined to the utmost, while respecting the individuality and local flavor of each brewery.The selling point of each sake is its smooth, full-bodied, and mellow taste. breweries.name: Hachinohe Shuzo
breweries.address: 9, Honchu, Minatomachi, Hachinohe Shi, Aomori Ken, 031-0812, Japan
breweries.description: During the Genbun era (1736–1741), the first-generation Komai Shozaburo set out from the Omi Province with lofty ambitions, eventually finding his path in sake brewing in the land of Mutsu. The brewery officially began its operations in An'ei 4 (1775) and is set to celebrate its 250th anniversary in 2025.All rice used in brewing is sourced exclusively from Aomori Prefecture, paired with the region’s original yeast strains. The brewing water is drawn from the renowned spring in the Kanisawa district of Hachinohe, ensuring the creation of an authentic local sake.In 2021, the brewery was ranked first among 643 breweries in the global sake brewery rankings. While honoring its long-standing traditions, the brewery embraces innovation to adapt to the times, continually challenging itself to create new possibilities. Business Hours:Monday to Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Winter hours)Open on Saturdays from October to February (*subject to temporary closures)Brewery Tours:Available starting at 10:00 AM, every hour (the 12:00 PM session is not available)Last tour starts at 3:00 PM

breweries.description: While our brewery was established in 1902, it wasn’t until 1951 that we adopted the brand name Nanbu Bijin. At that time, many Japanese sakes lacked the refined flavors and aromas that we see today. With a desire to create cleaner and more delicious sake, we named our brand Nanbu Bijin, drawing inspiration from the “Nanbu” region—known for its rich natural environment and abundant water—and the concept of a sake that embodies beauty and elegance (bijin). breweries.name: Fukurai
breweries.address: 5-31, Ubecho, Kuji Shi, Iwate Ken, 028-8111, Japan
breweries.description: In Meiji 40 (1907), when we decided to embark on a new journey in sake brewing, we were discussing potential names for the sake with our relatives.As we were struggling to come up with a good idea, a toddler named "Fuku," in the adorable stage of learning to walk, entered the room. Someone remarked, "Fuku has arrived," and the atmosphere instantly brightened.Inspired by this moment, we named the sake Fuku Kitaru with the hope that it would bring happiness to everyone who drinks it, sells it, or brews it. breweries.description: Founded in 1973 (Showa 48), Ichinokura was established through the collaboration of four breweries within the prefecture. In 1977 (Showa 52), the brewery launched Mukansa Honjozo Karakuchi as a second-class sake, which became a major hit. In 1983 (Showa 58), Ichinokura established the Ichinokura Sake University to promote the enjoyment of sake.The brewery has also been a pioneer in introducing innovative products, such as the low-alcohol sake Himezen in 1988 (Showa 63) and the sparkling sake Suzune in 1998 (Heisei 10). In 1993 (Heisei 5), a new headquarters and brewery were completed. Since 1994 (Heisei 6), Ichinokura has actively engaged in community-focused activities such as the Kurabiraki open house event and the Ichinokura Microbial Summer Camp.In recent years, the brewery has focused on improving working conditions, earning the Kurumin certification as a child-friendly company and promoting paternity leave for male employees. breweries.description: In 1906 (Meiji 39), Tahee Saito, the head of the Saito family, began brewing sake. During a prayer ceremony for the success of his brewing, the morning star was reflected through a sacred mirror onto a sake-filled issho masu (traditional wooden square cup). Taking this as a good omen, he named the brewery Kakuboshi (“Square Star”).Following the damage to the brewery caused by the Great East Japan Earthquake on March 11, 2011, the new Hakusan Brewery was established in August 2021.The Hakusan Brewery was built on the former site of Hakusan Elementary School. While the exterior retains the nostalgic appearance of the old school, the brewing equipment and machinery were completely updated. Additionally, new facilities for fruit wine production were installed. Currently, the brewery produces sake, liqueurs, and fruit wines. Same-day service is not available. If you wish to visit, please call at least one week in advance to confirm in advance.*We may not be able to accept the request due to the work schedule.

breweries.description: Since founded in 1661, the family has been brewing sake without interruption for nearly 350 years, making it the oldest sake brewery in Miyagi Prefecture. The family was founded when Oribe Uchigasaki I was ordered by Masamune Date, lord of the Sendai domain, to establish an inn in Tomiya, and served as the main camp. The sake brewing business was started by Sakuemon II. The sake brewing business was started by Sakuemon Sakuemon II, and the traditional cold brewing method is still practiced today, and the sake carefully brewed by the Nanbu a cheif sake maker has won gold medals many times at the National New Sake Competition. The name of the sake, "Hoyo," comes from the Chinese legend "Houming Chaoyang(when a phoenix cries on the morning of New Year's Day, it drives away misfortune and bestows good fortune)" by Li Zhenxian of the Tang Dynasty, and is a brand with good luck. Brewery tours are available (open June-August, reservations required).No tasting available.Open: 2nd and 4th Saturdays, Sundays, holidays, except Obon end-year and New Year's holidays.Sake can be purchased at the office on business days.

breweries.name: Aramasa Shuzou
breweries.address: 6-2-35, Omachi, Akita Shi, Akita Ken, 010-0921, Japan
breweries.description: Since its founding in 1852, Aramasa Shuzou has upheld deeply rooted traditions in sake brewing. One of our greatest points of pride is crafting junmai sake using Kyokai No. 6 yeast, an ancient sake yeast strain discovered in-house over 90 years ago. We also adopt the traditional kimoto method, established during the Edo period, which involves no added ingredients.We own 48 wooden brewing barrels (kioke), and all of our sake is crafted using these barrels. To preserve this traditional craft, we have established an in-house Kioke Workshop dedicated to the maintenance and restoration of wooden barrels.Additionally, in the Uyashinai district on the outskirts of Akita City, we promote pesticide-free cultivation over 30 hectares of farmland.Aramasa Sake Brewery is committed to preserving and nurturing Japan’s traditional fermentation culture, ensuring it is passed on to future generations.

For 200 yen, visitors can tour the ""Uchigura,"" a nationally registered tangible cultural property.In addition, visitors can sample sake and amazake (sweet sake) at the direct sales shop located next to the building.Open 10:00-16:00 (irregular holidays).For groups (10 or more people), please contact in advance.

Located on coastal sand dunes, it still boasts numerous springs and wells throughout the area, making it an ideal location for brewing.In the Meiji era, as many as 12 breweries were actively producing sake here. "Tenju" means to live happily Even at the age of a hundred years old, and is a wish for longevity to our customers. Based on the teachings of the company's predecessor that "sake brewing starts with rice cultivation", the Tenju Sake Rice Research Association was founded in 1983, ahead of other sake breweries in the country. Chokai for the brewing water, and with a commitment to rice, water, and technique, we continue to produce "Bishu Tenju", a sake loved by many people. breweries.description: Kuribayashi Shuzoutenwas founded in 1874 (Meiji 7) in Rokugo Village, Akita Prefecture (present-day Misato Town). The brewery is located in a region blessed with high-quality rice from the Senboku Plain and abundant underground water, selected as one of Japan’s Top 100 Famous Water sources.The brand name Harukasumi is said to have been inspired by a line from the Noh play Hagoromo.Our goal is to create sake that pairs well with everyday meals—sake meant to be enjoyed at the dinner table alongside delicious food. We primarily use contract-grown rice such as Misatonishiki and Kyokai No. 9 yeast to craft sake with a harmonious balance of aroma and flavor.

breweries.name: Hiraizumi Honpo
breweries.address: 59, Nakamachi, Hirasawa, Nikaho Shi, Akita Ken, 018-0402, Japan
breweries.description: It has been over 500 years since its establishment in 1487, the middle of the Muromachi period (1336-1573), when the Ginkakuji Temple was built in Kyoto. The sake brewing process is characterized by the traditional "Yamahai-brewing" method, in which natural lactic acid bacteria living inside the brewery are cultivated naturally instead of using chemical lactic acid, and the sake mother is carefully made over a period of one month. The brewery stubbornly adheres to the old-fashioned Yamahai brewing method with the creed, "We want to make sake that is more like a humble yet full-bodied plum blossom than a big cherry blossom", and continues to maintain the meticulous handcrafting that can only be achieved in a small brewery. Retail store available (weekdays 9:00-17:00)No brewery tours or store tastings

breweries.name: Ryouzeki Shuzou
breweries.address: 4-3-18, Maemori, Yuzawa Shi, Akita Ken, 012-0813, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1874 (Meiji 7), our brewery pioneered the "low-temperature long-term brewing method," a technique we shared openly with other brewers. This innovation laid the foundation for sake brewing in Tohoku, a region known for its cold climate.Our main house and four storehouses have been recognized as the first Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Akita Prefecture. Guided by the philosophy of Wajo Ryoshu—"Good sake is born from harmony among people, and it fosters harmony in return"—we continue to embrace traditional techniques while incorporating new ideas and methods.With a commitment to crafting sake that truly delights those who drink it, we remain dedicated to innovation and excellence. breweries.description: Yamagata City, the home of safflowers blessed with rich nature. Blessed with clear subterranean water from Mt. Zao and high-quality sake rice, we have been making traditional sake for more than 300 years since our establishment in the Kyoho era (1715 AD), making the most of the rich blessings of nature."Japanese heritage terroir brewing that symbolizes the traditional flavor of Yamadera and Yamagata's safflower culture".This is the commitment and theme of the Kotobukoraya Brewery's sake brewing process.We believe in pursuing better sake quality by focusing on sake rice, water, and the natural environment that nurtures them, and not being satisfied with the status quo while maintaining a sense of history.The quality of our sake has been awarded the top prize at the GI Yamagata "Yamagata Prefecture Kenjokai" Sake Tasting Competition", the Gold Prize at the National New Sake Competition, the Superior Prize at the Tohoku Sake Competition, and several other prestigious national and international awards. 9:30-11:30 or 13:00-16:00 on weekday business days (about 45 minutes. (Free to attend, tasting charged).Up to 20 people.Reservations must be made by phone at least one week prior to the desired tour date.Not available during peak winter season.

breweries.name: Furusawa Shuzou
breweries.address: 3-5-7, Marunochi, Sagae Shi, Yamagata Ken, 991-0023, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1836 (Tenpo 7), our brewery is located in Sagae City, Yamagata Prefecture. Nestled in a basin with the majestic Mt. Gassan of the Asahi mountain range to the west and the Zao ski resort in the Ouu mountain range to the east, the area is blessed with an abundance of soft water from Mt. Gassan’s spring water. This soft water creates a sake profile that is smooth and light, and it is in this environment that we have carried on sake brewing traditions for generations.Our fundamental philosophy is “Quality First.” We believe that making delicious sake relies on “land, ingredients, and people”—an embodiment of terroir. With over 180 years of tradition, honed craftsmanship, and the passion of our brewers, we have received the prestigious Superior Prize in the National Sake Appraisal for three consecutive years.Today, we produce sake, authentic shochu, and plum wine. Business Hours:10:00 AM – 4:00 PMClosed during New Year holidaysAdmission: FreePhone: 0237-86-5322Fax: 0237-86-0567 (available from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM)A tasting corner is available.The museum shop offers sake, sake lees, ochoko cups, and other items for purchase.For group visits, advance reservations are recommended for a smoother experience.

breweries.description: Founded in 1706. Oguni's sake rice is grown in an area rich in nature. Oguni Town is one of the heaviest snowfall areas in Japan, with snowfall ranging from 3 meters in the center of town to 5 meters in the mountains in winter.Because of this, Oguni Town has abundant water resources, and sake rice grown in the melted snow at the foot of the Iide Mountains has a different taste. Please try "Oguni Sakuragawa" made with sake rice grown with mineral-rich water. After registering at the sake brewery cafe "Kamosuk", visitors can watch a video on how sake is made and then take a guided tour of the brewery at the adjacent Sakuragawa Sake Brewery.This tour requires a reservation on the web at least 5 working days in advance.

breweries.name: Kousaka Shuzou
breweries.address: 7-3-10, Chuo, Yonezawa Shi, Yamagata Ken, 992-0045, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1923, this brewery is located in Yonezawa, a historic castle town associated with the Uesugi clan. Leveraging the frigid winters of snowy Yonezawa, the brewery adheres to the traditional methods of cold-season brewing, with meticulous attention to every detail. From washing the rice by hand to labeling the bottles, every step of the sake-making process is carried out with care and craftsmanship by skilled artisans.Patiently enduring the harsh winter while dreaming of the warmth of spring, their signature sake, Kobai, evokes the fragrant and elegant bloom of plum blossoms that herald the season’s arrival. With its crisp and refreshing flavor, this sake is truly a masterpiece that could only be crafted in the snowy region of Yonezawa. Brewery Tour Hours:10:00 AM – 4:00 PMFree tours and tastingsReservation required (please contact us)Direct Store Hours:10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Irregular holidays)

breweries.description: Founded in the Genroku era (around 1695), the company still insists on handmade products and wooden tools made of Yoshino cedar.The soaking vats, steamer, and warming barrels are also made of wood. They stay overnight in the middle of winter, in the snowy mountains of Yamagata, and make koji by transshipment by hand many times using traditional noodle lids. A pure rice brewery that honestly puts time and effort into sake brewing in a storehouse surrounded by the fragrance of wood, a nationally designated cultural property. Brewery tours are available on weekday afternoons only, reservations required.Tastings and sales available.

breweries.name: Tatenokawa Shuzou
breweries.address: 27, Mizuda, Yamadate, Sakata Shi, Yamagata Ken, 999-6724, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1832 (Tenpo 3), our brewery was named Tatenokawa by Lord Sakai of the Shonai Domain, who appreciated the sake's fresh and transparent flavor.In 2010, we transitioned all of our sake production to junmai daiginjo, focusing on creating the highest-quality sake. We place particular emphasis on high-precision rice polishing technology, crafting sake with a rice polishing ratio of 20% or less. This effort led to a historic milestone in the sake industry with the achievement of a rice polishing ratio of 1% for the first time ever.Additionally, we are exploring new horizons by incorporating wine fermentation techniques into sake brewing, continually challenging the future of sake.While preserving the transparent and refined flavors for which we are known, we remain committed to shaping the future of Japanese sake. Brewery Tours:Held every Saturday from November to May at 10:00 AM only.Tasting:Includes three varieties, including the high-end Extreme Series.Participation Fee:2,000 yen per adult (including tax) with a souvenir included.For more details, please visit the official Tatenokawa Sake Brewery website.

breweries.name: Yonetsuru Shuzou
breweries.address: 1076, Niijuku, Takahata Machi, Higashiokitama Gun, Yamagata Ken, 992-0301, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1697. A sake brewer with a history as an official sake brewer of the Uesugi clan of Yonezawa. Since 1983, the company has been engaged in sake brewing along the mountains of southern Yamagata Prefecture, starting from the cultivation of sake rice, and its quality is proven by the fact that it has been selected for JAL and ANA's business class in-flight sake. The company is also eager to produce new types of sake, such as dry sparkling sake and pink-colored junmai sake that uses no rice other than white rice.The brand name Yonetsuru has two meanings: gratitude and happiness. The store attached to the brewery is open on business days (weekdays and Saturdays only in December) only.Tours are available from April to September with advance notice.

breweries.description: In 1972, Yamagata Meizo Co., Ltd. was established as a joint bottling plant for five sake breweries in the Kitamurayama region of Yamagata Prefecture. 1985 saw the establishment of the Cooperative Association Rokkasen Sake Brewing Cooperative Association to handle the brewing division, and in 1992 the company name was changed to Rokkasen, Ltd.We aim of creating sake that gently resonates with people's hearts, like the waka poems composed by Rokkasen, an outstanding poet active in the Heian period (794-1192).From sake of ultra-dry to sparkling sake, we continue to challenge the possibilities of sake. breweries.name: Dewazakura Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-4-6, Hitoichimachi, Tendo Shi, Yamagata Ken, 994-0044, Japan
breweries.description: Since its founding, Dewazakura Shuzou has remained committed to crafting sake by hand, relying on the five senses of its brewers. Known as a pioneer in the sake industry, Dewazakura was among the first to introduce ginjo sake to the market, at a time when it was not yet commercially available in Japan.The brewery also led the way in exporting sake, becoming one of the first to expand internationally. Today, Dewazakura exports to over 35 countries worldwide.From our local community to people around the globe, we will continue to craft sake that can be enjoyed by everyone. Brewery Tours:Held regularly. For details and reservations, please check our website.We also invite you to visit the adjacent Dewazakura Museum.

breweries.description: We are a small sake brewery in Iwaki City, Fukushima PrefectureFounded in 1845 (Koka 2), Matabei I is said to have loved sake so much that he started brewing sake for himself. The name "Matabei" not only pays homage to his ancestors, but also expresses his will "to brew sake that is not flashy or elegant, but is as honest as a wild samurai.We produce "Matabei" in a brewery built in the Meiji era with the desire to preserve the traditional way of sake brewing and to make sake that will please the local people. breweries.description: Nihonmatsu, where water from the Adatara Mountains gushes forth in abundance.Founded in 1752, the Daishichi Sake Brewery has continued to brew rich, beautiful sake here, focusing exclusively on the sake brewing method of "Kimoto-zukuri".The combination of the traditional "Kimoto-zukuri" brewing technique and the innovative ultra-flat rice polishing technique has earned Daishichi a reputation both in Japan and around the world for its unique and distinctive sake that is a perfect accompaniment to meals.We will continue to carve out a new history of great sake. We offer paid sake brewery tours by reservation only, where you can enjoy the process of making sake.This is a luxurious tour course where you can taste 5~7 kinds of recommended Japan sake and liqueurs.

breweries.name: Akebono Shuzou
breweries.address: 2, Inui, Aizubange, Kawanuma Gun, Fukushima Ken, 969-6537, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1904. Winters are cold, summers are hot. It is a sake brewery located in Aizuzaka downtown, near the center of the Aizu Basin. While watching the growth of locally grown rice, you can feel the state of the rice firsthand, and brew sake by taking advantage of the characteristics of the rice. We aim for sake that has a personality that naturally exudes. While aiming to further improve the quality of sake, we will continue to face sake with all our hearts and souls without forgetting the uniqueness of Akebono Sake Brewery, and continue to make "sake that continues to evolve with the times and nature" and "sake that infinitely springs up with fun whether it is made or drunk". Currently, we do not allow visitors and do not sell directly to customers as we do not have a store.

breweries.name: Hanaharu Shuzou
breweries.address: 24-1, Omimae, Kozashimachi Nakashigo, Aizuwakamatsu Shi, Fukushima Ken, 965-0065, Japan
breweries.description: In the 3rd year of Kyoho (1718) during the reign of the eighth shogun, Prince Tokugawa Yoshimune, in a corner of the outer moat gate of Tsuruga Castle, Izutsuya Kuemon established a sake brewing business and named the sake "Tenshoshu". Later, in the role of Boshin (1869), which is famous for the White Tiger Corps, Izutsuya was also burned down in the war. While everyone was overwhelmed by grief, Izutsuya quickly rebuilt the brewery and changed the name of the sake to "Hanaharu" after the Chinese poem "Hanahahiraku-shukoku-no-haru", "to restore the brightness of flowers and the harmony of spring" to the heart. For more than 300 years since its founding, with the catchphrase "The goodness of Aizu is the goodness of sake", we are stubbornly and earnestly working sake brewing. Reservations required for tours. Weekdays only. Please contact us at least one week in advance. 9:00-11:30 or 13:00-16:00, approx. 40 min. Free tour, free tasting. The store is closed irregularly.

breweries.description: Homare Sake Brewery was established in 1918. We have been producing the Japanese Sake with careful stewardship and the grace of the ultra-soft spring water flowing from Mt. Iide.Our flagship Sake, Junmai Daiginjo Black Label won the title of IWC Champion Sake in 2015.We will proudly and constantly continue to follow through on our commitment and pursue the highest quality with the greatest passion and innovative sprit. We are particular about all rice used for over junmai sake and above is Aizu Gohyakumangoku, Aizu Gohyakumangoku contract with a local farmer to grow with low amounts of pesticides. This area has long been known for its high-quality rice and pristine spring water, which flows through granite known as mikage-ishi.Founded over 240 years ago by Omi merchants, we have continued to honor tradition while embracing a spirit of innovation, dedicating ourselves to sake brewing in this ideal location. breweries.name: Sudou Honke
breweries.address: 2125, Obara, Kasama Shi, Ibaraki Ken, 309-1701, Japan
breweries.description: All of our products meet the criteria for geographical indication. Each batch is brewed using traditional methods passed down through generations and is stored exclusively as unpasteurized sake. Our brewing water comes from spring water that has fed the same well for hundreds of years, and all rice used is sourced within a 5-kilometer radius of the brewery.Our Kimoto Junmai Daiginjo contains 410 μg of 5-aminolevulinic acid per 100 ml, a substance known to inhibit COVID-19. In 2020, it was awarded the highest gold prize at the New World section of the Catad'Or Wine Awards, and was highly praised as a "Burgundy white" by Robert Parker in his publication. All of our sake is junmai daiginjo with a polishing ratio of 50% or less, with the highest reaching 27%. Additionally, all of our sake is unfiltered. The brewery began producing sake using the rich water and high-quality rice from the fertile Satomi region, where agriculture and forestry were thriving.Believing that "sake is a refreshing tonic for the soul and a means of creating harmonious relationships, where people pour for each other," the brewery focuses on crafting sake that is enjoyed by people of all ages, with the motto of producing "delicious sake that everyone can enjoy." breweries.description: Founded in 1831. Founded by Yohei Sagara, who searched for the ideal water for sake brewing. The brewery is located in a natural setting surrounded by lush green mountains and rice paddies. The brewery uses the subsoil water from the Nikko mountain range that gushes out on the premises as its brewing water, which is characterized by its soft and clear texture that even has a sweet taste.The brewery cherishes the characteristics of this water, which the founder fell in love with, and uses Tochigi rice and Tochigi yeast to make Tochigi's local sake, as well as studying brewing techniques and brewing sake with love and care. breweries.name: Kobayashi Shuzou
breweries.address: 743-1, Soshima, Oyama Shi, Tochigi Ken, 323-0061, Japan
breweries.description: It's not about controlling, it's about understanding and expressing what's here. Everything important for sake brewing has always here since long ago. The sensitivity of the brewer is reflected as color in the time spun by nature and people. We would like to convey to everyone the "Hououbiden" that can be brewed only now."Hououbiden" is located in Nikko, Tochigi, along the Nikko-Nihonko-Keishi-Kaido road.Blessed with the abundant groundwater from the Mt. Nikko range and the magnificent and delicate nature, Hououmida has been brewing sake for 150 years since its establishment in 1872. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.name: Watanabe Sahei Shouten
breweries.address: 450, Imaichi, Nikko Shi, Tochigi Ken, 321-1261, Japan
breweries.description: A sake brewery established in 1842 and located in Nikko, a city famous for its World Heritage Site.We are carefully brewed with Nikko's famous water and harsh winter cold. The brewery places particular emphasis on junmai, with 90% of its production being junmai sake.In recent years, the brewery has been focusing on sake made with rice grown under contract with local farmers, and is making sake that is full of the charms of the region. Tours are available between 9:00-11:30 and 13:00-16:30 by appointment for a minimum of two people.The basic tour is free of charge, but for an English-speaking guide, the fee will be 500 yen per person.Tastings are always available to try several varieties with no reservation required.

breweries.name: Tentaka Shuzou
breweries.address: 2166, Hiruhata, Otawara Shi, Tochigi Ken, 324-0411, Japan
breweries.description: Since founded in 1914, the company has been making only dry sake, that "if it is not dry, it is not sake" and has been making "rice-only sake" for more than half a century since the days when the term "junmai-shu" did not exist. Currently, we have an agricultural corporation as a subsidiary company, and our employees grow their own rice while increasing the area planted every year. Our sake has been highly acclaimed in various domestic and international contests, and is exported to more than 20 countries as organic sake with organic certifications in Japan, the U.S., and Europe.We also use rice grown almost exclusively in the prefecture and are vegan certified. Store hours: 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Open all year round (except New Year's Day)Tastings: Free tastings of about 15 kinds available at all times.

breweries.address: 1452, Awanomiya, Oyama Shi, Tochigi Ken, 329-0201, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1872 (Meiji 5), this sake brewery is a nationally registered tangible cultural property in Oyama City, Tochigi Prefecture. The representative brand of the concept of local production for local consumption "Mongai Fushutu", which is still a small-scale product is consumed in Tochigi Prefecture even now, more than 90%. The brewery is proactively taking on new challenges, such as the industry's first brewing method, which is not bound by stereotypes.Based on the solid technology shown by our award-winning history, we will continue to take on new challenges in response to the times, in order to embody both tradition and innovation. Free and paid tastings available at our Wakamori Antenna Shop during business hours.Sake brewery and distillery tours available (advance reservations required through our website).*See website for details.

We proudly offer four unique sake varieties: Chidori, a beloved local brand; Kashin, a pink sake named after a novel by Ryotaro Shiba; Daiwaraku, inspired by the words of poet Shinmin Sakamura; and Usagi no Ballerina, crafted with yeast derived from Sakurahime, the official flower of Ehime Prefecture. breweries.description: The brand concept is "old and new manufacturing". Our policy is to cultivate our own rice, in which all raw rice is harvested from rice paddies on the same water vein as the brewing water (groundwater), and the key concepts are natural x traditional. All the Senkin is bottled the next day without any modification (all bottled), and although it is undiluted, it has an alcohol content of 15% or less.Using a wooden tub, raw rice is prepared as a principle, and the traditional manufacturing method is followed.Based on sourness and sweetness, it is designed with the image of a sweet, sour and juicy taste. breweries.address: 178-2, Otsuka, Ogawa Machi, Hiki Gun, Saitama Ken, 355-0328, Japan
breweries.description: Brewing sake with the bounty of natureSince its establishment in 1902, Seiun Sake Brewery has been brewing quality sake with the aim of creating sake loved by the local community. The groundwater of Ogawa-machi is abundantly supplied from the surrounding mountain, and the quality of the water is said to be comparable to "Nada no Miyamizu (Famous spring in Kansai region)". The town is also known as "Kanto Nada (Nada of the East)" as one of the best brewing areas in the Kanto region. The cold winter temperatures unique to the basin nurtures Ogawa washi (traditional Japanese paper) which is ideal for ginjo brewing. Seiun Brewery also actively purchases rice produced in Saitama Prefecture, much of which is harvested in the local surrounding area. For the past 30 years, local pesticide-free rice has also been used, and this rice is carefully polished by the company's own milling process to become the raw material for sake brewing.Seiun is our sake, created from rice lovingly grown by local farmers and carefully brewed by our toji (a chief sake maker), taking advantage of the town's nature blessings. breweries.address: 1916, Kanzaki Honjuku, Kanzaki Chou, Katori Gun, Chiba Ken, Japan
breweries.description: In 1689, Nabeshiten started brewing sake in a warehouse in front of Naritasan Shinshoji Temple, but has since moved to Kozakimachi, Katori-gun, where the countryside is more rural. The brewing of sake has long been done by Niigata Touji and Nanbu Touji, but in 1997, the brewery began brewing sake entirely by its own staff in order to produce sake with its own unique characteristics.From the "birth" of sake brewing (steamed rice, koji, and mash) to the "growth" of the sake (oroshi, filtration, hi-ire, storage, and bottling), all processes are carried out by our own staff.Our first priority is to ship products that many of our customers find "Delicious!".Sake is between people, and sake is between the heart and the mind. It is my mission to create sake that resonates with people's hearts. Please choose from three brewery tours at 11:00, 13:00, and 15:00 daily.Reservations required.Tel:0478-79-0161 (Higashi Kura store) or e-mail: kura@nabedana.co.jpWatch a video → Observe sake brewing process → Tasting.Time required: 60-90 minutes. Tasting: 1,200 yen/person (charged)

breweries.description: Takara Shuzo's Matsudo Plant, which has an area equivalent to about three times the size of the Tokyo Dome, is the main plant in the East Japan region, manufacturing more than 500 items of its mainstay products, including Kou-type shochu, chuhai, and hon mirin (Japanese rice wine).The plant is equipped with one of the largest continuous distillation machines in Japan, and the high-grade alcohol produced at this facility is used as an ingredient in Takara Shuzo products.The plant also has 12 filling lines for bottling sake, shochu, chuhai, and hon mirin, as well as providing safe, reliable, and high-quality products. The flagship brand "Kasen" is named after the joy of discovering a well on the brewery grounds that is ideal for sake brewing. The brewery's family motto is "Brew with care, sell with care." With a spirit of innovation that captures the essence of the times, Tamura Shuzoujou continues to create sake that reflects its unique character and tradition. breweries.description: Ishikawa Sake Brewery is a Tokyo sake brewery that brews "Tamajiman" of sake and "Tama no Megumi" and "TOKYO BLUES" of craft beer.It has a historical museum, Italian and Japanese restaurants, and a direct sales store. There are six buildings registered as tangible cultural properties, and a 700-year-old sacred tree watches over the premises. Thanks Day is held on the fourth Saturday of every month and on consecutive Sundays and national holidays.Bread, sweets, and local pickles made with limited-edition sake, sake cake, and other items typical of a sake brewery are sold, and tours are available by appointment. Visits can be available at any time.Please check the hours of operation of the site facilities in advance on the official website.https://www.tamajiman.co.jp/Tastings are not available.Tours are available on a monthly appreciation day for a fee-based tour (prices vary depending on the tour).

breweries.name: Noguchi Shuzouten
breweries.address: 2-4-8, Kotobuki-cho, Fuchu Shi, Tokyo, 183-0022, Japan
breweries.description: In Fuchu City, Tokyo—once home to the provincial government of Musashi Province—Noguchi Shuzouten began its journey in 1860 (Manen 1).This was the closing chapter of the Edo period, a time of significant transformation as Japan transitioned to the Meiji era. It was the era when historical figures such as Katsu Kaishu, Yukichi Fukuzawa, and John Manjiro sailed to America aboard the Kanrin Maru, and the assassination of Tairo Naosuke Ii in the Sakuradamon Incident shook the nation.The Noguchi family, who served as Jinin (shrine officials) at Okunitama Shrine, the main shrine of Musashi Province, laid the foundation for the brewery. Under the name Nakaya, the first-generation Noguchi Kyubei began brewing sake, initially producing sacred offerings for the shrine.However, rapid urbanization during the late Showa era forced the brewery to pause its production at its original facility. Despite this, they have revived the tradition of crafting jizake—local sake from Fuchu, the heart of Musashi Province, and the greater Tokyo region.Enjoy these handcrafted sakes that harmoniously blend tradition and innovation.

breweries.name: Ozawa Shuzou
breweries.address: 2-770, Sawai, Ome Shi, Tokyo To, 198-0172, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1702 (Genroku 15) in Sawai, a village on the trade route connecting Edo and Koshu, Sawanoi has been cherished for over 300 years as a local sake of Okutama, Tokyo.Although Okutama is part of Tokyo, it is characterized by its deep mountains and gorges. The area of Sawai, where Sawanoi originated, is known as a "village of fine water" due to its pristine and abundant streams. The name Sawanoi derives from this place name.Around the brewery, visitors can enjoy various attractions, including Sawanoi-en, a spot offering light meals and souvenirs, and Kikizake-dokoro, where you can savor the fine sake of Sawanoi. There are also establishments such as Mamagotoya and Mameraku, which serve tofu and yuba made with Sawanoi's brewing water, and CAFE Shizuku, known for its coffee brewed with the same pure water. These facilities attract many visitors year-round. Sake brewery tours are by appointment only, please make reservations thru web.https://reserva.be/sawanoikengakuFor groups of 11 or more, please contact us by phone.Closed on Mondays (or Tuesday if Monday is holiday), New Year holidays, and other irregular holidays.

The brewery has continued to brew sake in a rich natural setting, with a national park and Nanasawa Onsen hot springs in the vicinity.In recent years, the reputation of its ginjo-shu has been growing, with its daiginjo sake winning the gold medal at the National New Sake Competition for three consecutive years.We have also begun growing sake rice locally and continue to take on new challenges. breweries.name: Kubota Shuzou
breweries.address: 702, Negoya, Midori Ku, Sagamihara Shi, Kanagawa Ken, 252-0153, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1844 (Koka 1), Kubota Shuzou uses the pristine spring water of the Tanzawa mountain range to produce its sake, Sagaminada. The area surrounding the brewery remains as rich in natural beauty as it was at the time of its founding. In spring, bamboo shoots and wild vegetables thrive, while in summer, fireflies light up the night—a scene so picturesque that the location has been used for film shoots.The brewing of Sagaminada is based on faithful adherence to the fundamentals of ginjo brewing. However, the ginjo aroma is kept mild and natural, allowing the umami of the rice to take center stage. The result is a well-balanced sake crafted to pair seamlessly with meals.While preserving the legacy of this historic brewery, we strive to be a next-generation sake producer, continually pursuing greater levels of excellence in flavor. Business Hours:10:00 AM – 5:00 PMOpen year-roundParking:AvailableBrewery Tour:Available by reservation, 500 yen per person- Tasting of approximately 10 varieties is provided after the tour.- Basic English guidance available

breweries.address: 662, Uchinomachi, Nishi Ku, Niigata Shi, Niigata Ken, 950-2112, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in the first year of Taisho era (1912) on the title land. In Uchino-cho, where our company is located, you can overlook the multi-level crossing of the Shinkawa River, which was excavated by human power during the Edo period, and feel the history of Niigata's rich granary area that has been built.Our brewing water is groundwater filtered by sand in the dune area, and our sake brewing takes full advantage of Niigata's climate.At recent years, in order to make local sake familiar to people around the world, we have been actively working on the development of new Japan sake and local manufacturing guidance overseas. Reservations required.Fridays from May to September, 10:00-12:00 and 13:00-15:00.

breweries.name: Sasaiwai Shuzou
breweries.address: 3249, Matsuno, Nishikan Ku, Niigata Shi, Niigata Ken, 953-0015, Japan
breweries.description: Licensed in 1899, this brewery is beloved in its hometown of Nishikan Ward, Niigata City, where much of its sake is enjoyed as a local favorite. All rice used is grown in Niigata City, creating sake that reflects the character of the region. While we continue to brew classic, everyday sake cherished by the local community, we also pursue “creative brewing,” experimenting with new ideas each year.The original wooden and earthen-walled brewery building has been renovated and opened as a direct sales shop for our sake. Additionally, we’ve established a "Koji Classroom," a space for hands-on koji-making experiments and various workshops. Business Hours:Weekdays 9:00 AM – 5:00 PMWeekends and Holidays 10:00 AM – 5:00 PMPurchases and tastings of Japanese sake are available. Brewery tours and salt-koji making workshops are offered by reservation.

breweries.name: LAGOON BREWERY
breweries.address: 576-1,Maeshinden Kita ku,Niigata shi, Niigata,950-3324,Japan
breweries.description: Nestled beside Fukushima Lagoon in northern Niigata Prefecture—an area renowned as one of Japan's premier stopover sites for swans and designated as one of "Japan's 100 Best Natural Landscapes"—a small craft sake brewery was established in 2021. The brewery also features a café and offers guided tours.Uniquely, the brewery holds both a license for producing craft sake (classified as other brewed alcoholic beverages) and a license for producing Japanese sake exclusively for export, a rarity in the industry. Guided by the motto of creating "A diverse range of flavors that evoke delight and emotion," the brewery is committed to sustainability. It experiments with natural cultivation rice and unconventional ingredients such as kuromoji (spicebush) and tomatoes.Products: Craft sake (classified as other brewed alcoholic beverages in Japan), Japanese sake (export only)Brand name: Shokuu [Shop & Cafe]We have both a shop and a cafe.Business Hours:10:00 AM – 5:00 PMClosed:Mondays (or the following day if Monday is a public holiday)Note:Hours may change unexpectedly due to sake production.Tasting: Free for small samples. There are also paid tastings available in the cafe space where you can enjoy the sake at your leisure.[Brewery Tours]From the large windows of the attached Shop & Cafe, you can freely view the inside of the brewery (fermentation room) during business hours.Note:Access to the interior of the brewery is not permitted.[Paid Brewery Tours (Entry into Brewery)]Tours are available by reservation.For more details, please check our official website.

breweries.name: Ishimoto Shuzou
breweries.address: 847-1, Kitayama, Konan Ku, Niigata Shi, Niigata Ken, 950-0116, Japan
breweries.description: Kameda Township is located almost in the center of Niigata City. The dune land surrounded by the Agano River, the Shinano River, and the Little Agano River that connects the two is the home of Koshino Kanbai.Kameda Township is blessed with an abundance of good water from the Agano River and winter snows that create a low-temperature environment, making it truly an ideal location for sake brewing. At Kameda-go, where is also famous for plum blossoms, enduring the cold, this plum blossom, which gives off a dignified beauty, is the origin of the name Koshino Kanbai.Ishimoto Shuzo continues to brew Koshino kanbai here today. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.name: Chiyonohikari Shuzou
breweries.address: 656, Kubomatsubara, Myoko Shi, Niigata Ken, 944-0061, Japan
breweries.description: In order to pursue quality-oriented and customer-oriented in all of our products, Chiyo no Hikari Sake Brewery brews sake using the rich natural environment of Joetsu and the technology cultivated through tradition and innovation.Reconcile old-fashioned technologies and methods with new challenges.1. The challenge of making raw rice brewed with natural lactic acid.2. Challenge to fermentation in closed tanks in the second half of Moromi.We will continue to study old knowledge, find new knowledge and reasoning from it, and continue to challenge ourselves to reach new heights. breweries.description: Joetsu Shuzou is a historic sake producer with a legacy spanning over 220 years.We strive to craft sake that retains the beloved flavors cherished by our local community while appealing to a wider audience.Sake is there for us in every moment—whether in times of joy, sorrow, or celebration. It uplifts the spirit, offers comfort, and accompanies us like a close friend, resonating with our emotions.We remain committed to creating sake that connects deeply with people's hearts, like a trusted companion. That is the kind of sake we will continue to produce. Business Hours:9:00 AM – 5:00 PMIf you wish to participate in brewery tours or tastings:1. Please contact us in advance by phone or through our website.2. Arrive by 4:00 PM.3. Some tastings may incur a fee.

breweries.name: Hakkai jouzou
breweries.address: 1051, Nagamori, Minamiuonuma Shi, Niigata Ken, 949-7112, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1922 in the Uonuma region of Niigata Prefecture, one of the heaviest snowfall areas in Japan.Since its establishment, the company has consistently aimed for the highest quality sake brewing using "Raiden-sama no Shimizu", an extremely soft water blessed by Hakkaisan (Mt. Hakkaisan), to create a light and delicate taste sake that enhances communication with food". The company taking advantage of the wisdom of heavy snowfall areas, continues to take on challenges in new fields, such as products using the "Hakkaisan Snow Chamber" filled with 1,000 tons of snow, and "Amasake" based on the theme of rice, koji, fermentation, and water. Sake brewery tours are not accepted, but at "Uonuma no Sato", where the second Kouwagura that makes regular sake is located, you can visit the snow storage of the "Hakkaisan Snow Room" and sample our alcoholic beverages.At Sarukurayama Brewery, you can enjoy beer while looking at the brewery through the glass window.
breweries.visiting_info: Sake brewery tours are not accepted, but at "Uonuma no Sato", where the second Kouwagura that makes regular sake is located, you can visit the snow storage of the "Hakkaisan Snow Room" and sample our alcoholic beverages.At Sarukurayama Brewery, you can enjoy beer while looking at the brewery through the glass window.

breweries.name: Yukitsubaki Shuzou
breweries.address: 3-14, Nakamachi, Kamo Shi, Niigata Ken, 959-1351, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1806, Kamo City, where the brewery is located, has long been called the "Little Kyoto of Hokuetsu" and is famous for its native colonies of snow camellias. The name "Koshino Yukitsubaki" comes from the dedication of the snow camellia, which endures harsh winters and blooms beautifully in the spring, and it is this dedication that we hope to embody in our sake brewing. Since 2011, we have been a "junmai brewery" that specializes in high-quality junmai, junmai-ginjo, and junmai-dai-ginjo sake, and while maintaining our traditional handmade brewing methods, we are also actively introducing brewing equipment that incorporates the latest technology to keep pace with new changes in our daily sake production. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.name: Kanemasu Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-9-30, Yutakacho, Shibata Shi, Niigata Ken, 957-0016, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1822, the brewery is located in Shibata City, Niigata Prefecture, a region blessed with abundant underground water from the Iide Mountain Range and known for its excellent rice production.Taking advantage of this rich environment, the brewery's brewmaster and brewery workers cultivate sake rice in their own fields, ensuring an integrated approach from raw material production to brewing. The brewery produces the Hatsuhana pure rice sake series and the Kanemasu standard series.In addition to brewing sake, the brewery also distills shochu. One of their unique offerings, Kanemasu Aka Label, is a sake brewed using the hashira shochu method, incorporating their in-house rice shochu into the brewing process. breweries.description: Echigo Zakura Brewery is located along National Route 49, not far from "Hyouko Lake".The brewery was founded in 1890 in Mizuhara-cho (now Agano City).Enjoy the excellent sake brewed with rice and water nurtured by Niigata's great nature, and brewed by brewers using traditional techniques with great care.Its representative brand is "Daiginjo Echigozakura". breweries.name: Kusumi Shuzou
breweries.address: 1537-2, Ojimaya, Nagaoka Shi, Niigata Ken, 949-4511, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1833. Seisen's representative brand is sake carefully brewed with water from the mountains behind the brewery (designated as Niigata Prefecture's famous water) and the traditional techniques of the Echigo chief sake maker, based on the principle that sake is created by the local rice, water, human kindness, and nature. Among them, Junmai Daiginjo "Kame-no-o" is brewed with "Kame-no-o" rice, which is a phantom rice that ceased to exist before World War II, and which was revived and home-grown over a period of three years in 1980 from a mere 1500 seeds (the first postwar brewery to do so).This episode became the motif for the manga "Natsuko-no-Sake," which was later dramatized for TV and broadcast overseas. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.name: DHC Shuzo
breweries.address: 1-6-1, Kayama, Kita Ku, Niigata Shi, Niigata Ken, 950-3322, Japan
breweries.description: This sake brewery has been making sake in Kita-ku, Niigata City (formerly Toei City) for over 100 years. In 2014, it became a member of the DHC Group and was reborn as DHC Sake Brewery. The brewery's goal is to create sake that is rooted in the local community, and 100% of the rice is grown in Niigata Prefecture. Since its establishment in 1908, the brewery has been using modern facilities with skilled techniques and traditional manufacturing methods to produce sake with a focus on freshness.We continue to pursue even more delicious sake by acquiring "Kosher" certification, which has a 450-year history as a system that guarantees food safety. You can enjoy tasting and shopping at the tourist store "Echigo-no-Sato Kayama-Tei" adjacent to the sake brewery.Visitors are welcome to use the restroom only.10:00-17:00 (closed mainly on Thursdays), 11-minute walk from JR Toyosaka Station.Storehouse tours are available for a minimum of four people; please consult with us at least one month in advance of the desired date.

breweries.description: Founded in 1830 (Tenpo 1) in Asahi, Nagaoka City, Niigata Prefecture, a region surrounded by rice fields and woodlands, Asahi Shuzou has been crafting sake for over 190 years. The brewery uses soft, pristine groundwater from the local aquifer, high-quality sake rice cultivated through collaborative research with regional farmers, and techniques passed down from the Koshiji Toji group, combined with technological innovations rooted in fundamental research.Dedicated to producing sake that reflects its Niigata origins, Asahi Shuzou adheres to a policy of prioritizing quality and putting customers first. For details about the brewery tour, please check the following URL:https://www.asahi-shuzo.co.jp/visit/brewery/Tasting is available at the retail store "Sara no Sato Asahi Yama" in front of Asahi Shuzo.Business Hours:10:00 AM – 6:00 PM

breweries.name: Yahiko Shuzou
breweries.address: 1830-1, Kamiizumi, Yahiko Mura, Nishikambara Gun, Niigata Ken, 959-0321, Japan
breweries.description: Our brewery serves Yahiko Shrine, dedicated to Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto, a descendant of the sun goddess Amaterasu. We use the spring water from Mt. Yahiko as our brewing water and exclusively source rice, including Yamadanishiki, from Yahiko Village.Since our founding in 1838, we have upheld the family creed: “Do not focus on quantity; always strive for pure and refined brewing.” Guided by this principle, we maintain meticulous brewing practices such as long-term low-temperature fermentation and bottle pasteurization, producing small-batch, handcrafted sake with the utmost care. Brewery Tours: Not availableSales & Tasting: Not available

breweries.name: Hakuro Shuzou
breweries.address: 1927, Tokamachi, Nagaoka Shi, Niigata Ken, 940-1131, Japan
breweries.description: Since founded in 1751, the brewery has been at one end of part of the food culture of sake in Echigo Nagaoka (Nagaoka City, Niigata Prefecture), passing through a history of drastic change.In 1882, we took over the sake brewery of the Makino family, the feudal lord of the Nagaoka domain from which the name "Hakuro" was derived, and became a "Daimyo-kura," using the family's "Mitsukashiwa" crest and inheriting the product name "Hakuro" .The people involved in sake brewing have changed over time, but our company's stance of "producing delicious sake that will be appreciated by many people" has been consistently passed down to the present. breweries.description: The company was founded in 1650, the 3rd year of Keian. The sake name "Kaga no I" was named by Toshitsune Maeda, the third generation of the Kaga Maeda family, and has been carefully passed down to this day since then. In response to the Great Fire in Itoigawa City Station Kita that occurred in December 2016, a newly constructed sake brewery was completed in the spring of 2018 and sake production resumed.Shinzo has become able to better determine the condition of the raw rice, and is currently pursuing a transparent taste while considering the compatibility of the brewing water of "medium-hard water", which is a characteristic of our own brewery, based on the belief that "making sake is nurturing, and making sake that is faithful to the basics" while taking advantage of the characteristics of this brewery. 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Please contact us in advance for Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays.The tour takes about 30 minutes including tasting.Parking for large buses is available.Please make a reservation at least 2 days in advance for a tasting and information from our staff.

breweries.description: A sake brewery founded in 1881 in Shibata City, Kita Echigo. With "Kikusui Funaguchi", Japan's first draft sake in a can, as its representative brand, the brewery also produces seasonal nigorizake "Gorohachi", "Kikusui no Karakuchi" and "Kuramitsu" a jummai-daiginjo-shu (daiginjo-shu) sake. The Kikusui Institute for Sake Culture is located on the premises, and is dedicated not only to sake brewing, but also to the pursuit of "kotozukuri", the art of making sake more interesting and offering new ways to enjoy sake. breweries.description: Our company is a new sake brewery founded in 2011 with the aim of passing on to the future the skills and culture of the "Kubiki chief sake maker" tradition in the former town of Yoshikawa (now Yoshikawa Ward, Joetsu City).The brewery continues to produce "sake with umami" by using the "traditional techniques" handed down from generation to generation, with high-quality "sake rice" produced locally in Yoshikawa and "water" enriched by the beech trees on the sacred Mt. Ogamidake.In 2023, our representative brand "Yoshikawa Toji Daiginjo" won a prize at the National New Sake Competition, and "Junmai Umeshu" won a bronze prize at the National Umeshu Competition. Our company is a sake brewery with a directly managed store located at "Roadside Station: Yoshikawa Toji no Sato" in Joetsu City, Niigata Prefecture.Visitors are free to tour the brewery from inside the store through the window, and upon request, we provide explanations of the brewery and the sake brewing process.

breweries.description: Founded in 1890. It is located in a town at the foot of a mountain called Mt. Kakuda, about an hour's drive from the center of Niigata City.Aiming to be a delicious sake that would please people, we chose the name "Tsurukame" to create an easy-to-understand and auspicious trademark. The brand "Tsurukame" is very auspicious and appreciated on a special day in each season, and is used for Father's Day, Respect for the Aged Day, and other celebrations in various places.Our brewery strives to produce the highest quality sake that will satisfy our customers, and we are particular about brewing in small batches dare. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.name: Kiminoi Shuzou
breweries.address: 3-11, Shimomachi, Myoko Shi, Niigata Ken, 944-0048, Japan
breweries.description: Kiminoi Sake Brewery has been refining the mountain waste brewing, which takes time and effort to make, for more than 100 years. Mountain waste brewing is one of the manufacturing methods that ferments with natural lactic acid bacteria over time, and is characterized by a deep taste. Our brewery is also inhabited by lactic acid bacteria that promote the mysterious activity of "life". Rather than adding commercially available lactic acid bacteria, we nurture these natural lactic acid bacteria and increase the number of healthy yeasts to create a high-quality sake mother (the source of sake). At our brewery, we call this "lactic acid bacteria brewing" and inherit the "elegant mountain waste" full of unique flavor. In addition to mountain waste, we also make modern construction, and pursue a variety of "umami" that matches the times. We will continue to make sake that will be loved with the times and will remain in everyone's memory. 9:00-16:00 *Free visit, no reservation required.Closed: Saturdays, Sundays, and national holidays. (confirmation required)For more information, please see the website.

breweries.name: Higashi Shuzou
breweries.address: 35, Nodamachi, Komatsu Shi, Ishikawa Ken, 923-0033, Japan
breweries.description: Shinsen's sake brewery, founded in 1860, was designated as a nationally registered tangible cultural property in 2009, and in 2017 was recognized as a component of the Japanese Heritage of Komatsu City's stone culture, "The Story of Walking with Pearl".The brewery's motto is to ensure that all elements involved in sake brewing are produced in Ishikawa Prefecture, and water, which is important for sake brewing, is sourced from underground water in the Hakusan Mountains and brewed with local sake rice and local yeast.Our goal is to create sake that is uniquely Ishikawa's local sake. This tour allows you to explore a sake brewery with over 160 years of history, including the stone warehouses designated as Registered Tangible Cultural Properties and the brewing process using the divine spring water, with explanations provided by the brewery staff. After the tour, tastings and retail sales are available at the shop (fee applies).・Estimated Tour TimeThe brewery tour takes approximately 30 minutes, followed by the tasting and retail sales, making a total of about 50 minutes.Note: Tour schedules may change during the brewing season. Please contact us for details.・Important Information* Reservations for tours must be made by email.* Tours are available for groups of 4 or more (for groups of fewer than 4, the fee for 4 people will be charged).* Tour fee is 1,100 yen per person (includes a 500-yen voucher for purchases).

breweries.name: Sougen Shuzou
breweries.address: 24-22, Horyumachisogen, Suzu Shi, Ishikawa Ken, 927-1225, Japan
breweries.description: Sogen Shuzou, founded in 1768 (Meiwa 5) during the mid-Edo period, is located in Suzu City at the tip of the Noto Peninsula in Ishikawa Prefecture, a region in the northern central part of Honshu, Japan. With over 250 years of history, it is a brewery steeped in tradition.In sake brewing, the craftsmen who oversee the brewing process are known as toji. The Noto Toji, who work in breweries across Japan, are considered one of the nation's four major toji groups, and Sogen is often regarded as their birthplace.Sogen Shuzou has endured significant challenges, including the Noto Peninsula Earthquake on January 1, 2024, and the Noto Peninsula Heavy Rainfall on September 21–22 of the same year. Despite these adversities, we continue to brew sake as we do every year, maintaining our dedication to tradition and quality. Business Hours:Monday to Friday 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (excluding certain public holidays)Closed on: Only during specific public holidays.Tasting and brewery tours: Not available.

breweries.description: Located in Hakusan City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Shata Shuzou sits at the foot of the sacred Mt. Hakusan, in the heart of the Kaga Plain, where fertile lands yield exceptional rice. From this land blessed by nature, we produce our regional sake brands, Tengumai and GoRIN.Founded in 1823 (Bunsei 6), we have been dedicated to crafting sake by hand that pairs beautifully with meals and delights the palate. Our mission is to share the essence of Japanese culture—sake—with the world while creating brews that bring pride and joy to our local community.We aim to continue crafting sake that nourishes the spirit, connects people, and fosters meaningful bonds. On-site Store "Tengumai CRAFT SAKE SHOP mau."Business Hours:10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (last order at 3:45 PM)Closed on: Sundays.Brewery Tours:Not available.

breweries.description: Founded in Meiji 2, with the management philosophy of "Brewing Noto", we manufacture Japan sake, soy sauce, and liqueurs that are particular about raw materials from Noto. Since 2014, we have been working with contract farmers to grow rice using abandoned farmland in Noto, returning 26 hectares to paddy fields, and in 2020 we achieved sake brewing with 100% Noto rice. We are promoting work style reform and working on manufacturing centered on young people that takes advantage of their spirit of challenge and flexibility. Since its founding in Matsuoka, Eiheiji-cho, blessed with good water, by the first sake brewery owner, Nizaemon Ishidaya, we have pursued sake brewing that is unique to Kuroryu Sake Brewery.Based on the philosophy of "making good sake", we have always explored new possibilities for sake, and have been a pioneer in the industry.Our goal is to create a sake with a delicate umami that brings out the flavor of the ingredients themselves, Yet, it has just the right amount of flavor and complements the food.Yet it has a moderate flavor, and elegant taste that seems to be close to the local food of Fukui. Products are available for purchase at Ishidaya, a retail store adjacent to the brewery.Please note that brewery tours and tastings are not available.Business hours: 10:00-16:30, Closed: every Sunday (with occasional holidays)

breweries.description: This sake brewery was founded in 1853, and its representative brand,“Hakugakusen” uses rice suitable for sake brewing from Fukui Prefecture and subterranean water from the Hakusan water vein underground water.We only brew pure rice sake, and express the people, land, and climate of Fukui to the fullest through our sake as Fukui Terroir.Additionally, the buildings, such as the store, main building, rice storehouse, and storehouse, which were built from the mid-Meiji period to the early Showa period, and are designated as nationally registered tangible cultural properties. breweries.address: 1-11, Yoshiecho, Sabae Shi, Fukui Ken, 916-0001, Japan
breweries.description: In 1860, our ancestors, who had been money changers and village headmen, started sake production. The name "Kichihei" has been passed down from generation to generation, and we are now in the 11th generation of this long-established business. The brewery brews only pure rice sake with no additives, using one of the whitest rice in Japan, with an average rice polishing ratio of 34% in the brewery. At the same time, all sake is aged in ice temperatures for a maximum of 10 years or more, or at least one year in negative temperatures, it is shipped after the real flavor is on it. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.description: Located at the foot of the Yatsugatake Mountains in Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture, our small brewery is blessed with abundant mountains and water sources. Founded in 1865 (Keio 1), we produce both sake and junmai shochu.The name Takenoi originates from the first brewery owner, Buzaemon Shimizu, and the well that draws pure spring water from the Yatsugatake mountain range.For brewing, we use Yamanashi-grown rice as well as premium sake rice sourced from across Japan. The yeast essential for fermentation primarily includes flower yeast strains developed by Tokyo University of Agriculture. breweries.description: In 1848, our brewery began as a small sake brewery called Goshinshu Sake Brewery. At that time, a large amount of old coins were excavated from the brewery grounds, which is why we were also known as "Kosen-ya" (old coins store).Here at the southern foot of Mt. Yatsugatake, the blessings of magnificent nature and clean spring water have supported our daily sake brewing in the past and still do today.Sake goes hand in hand with food culture. Food is rooted in geography, history, and climate, It has changed with the times. We, too, are always striving to make "good sake".We brew sake that reflects the climate, valuing what changes and what does not change.People-friendly, nature-friendly sake brewing.Sake that takes root in the land of Kai, that is TANIZAKURA. breweries.address: 2283, Hakushucho Daigahara, Hokuto Shi, Yamanashi Ken, 408-0312, Japan
breweries.description: Japan sake brewery founded in 1750. Based on the concept of "local sake" that is only possible when "local water, local rice, and local people" come together, Japan sake is brewed using sake rice grown by local contract farmers as the main ingredient by taking water from the Oshirakawa River vein, one of the 100 best waters in Japan. Representative brands are "Fuurin Bizan", "TBirodo-no-Aji", "Kinu-no-Aji" and "Ōnakaya". In addition, there are many products such as "Yama-no-kasumi", a sparkling Japan sake that uses the secondary fermentation method in the bottle (the same manufacturing method as champagne). breweries.description: In 1866, our company was founded in the Takatoo-chou, which is famous for its beautiful cherry blossoms.Inadani is surrounded by the Southern and Central Alps, making it an ideal environment for sake brewing.We use melted snow water from the Southern Alps, and this mineral-rich water influences the quality and taste of our sake.Sake that evokes the bounty of local nature is produced through the fusion of carefully selected ingredients and techniques, and is characterized by the depth of its flavor.By taking advantage of the beautiful natural environment of the region and combining tradition and technology, we aim to contribute to the region and sake culture through our passion for sake brewing. We do not accept tours or tastings for the general public, but you are welcome to visit the brewery's directly operated store.The store is open Monday through Friday from 9:00 am to 4:30 pm.

breweries.name: Daishinshuu Shuzou
breweries.address: 2380, Shimadachi, Matsumoto Shi, Nagano Ken, 390-0852, Japan
breweries.description: Daishinshu is a sake brewery located in Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture.The brewery’s brewing water comes from the snowmelt of the Northern Alps, which journeys underground over many years before reemerging. This water is known for its soft mouthfeel and delicate texture, lending Daishinshu's sake a clean, refreshing, and smooth flavor that reflects the purity of its source.The sake rice is cultivated in partnership with contract farmers primarily in Matsumoto. Surrounded by mountains, the Shinshu region experiences significant temperature differences between day and night and maintains low humidity, creating ideal conditions for producing healthy rice rich in umami.Crafted by skilled brewers who have honed their techniques over the years, Daishinshu's sake captures the essence of the natural environment, embodying the blessings of Shinshu’s unique climate and landscape in every drop—a truly divine sake.
