breweries.description: With the majestic Mt.Ariake towering before you amidst the beautiful rural landscape of Azumino, the sake brewed from locally sourced rice and the brewing water drawn from within the brewery, "Daisekkei," is a delight no matter which one you choose, or when, where, and with whom you enjoy it—it's always delicious.The brewery's direct store, "Kamon Daisekkei," is housed in a restored building from the late Edo period, featuring an inner sitting room and a garden for visitors to enjoy. Inside, we offer seasonal sake, sweets, and fun merchandise, available year-round without a day off.As part of your journey through Azumino, we warmly invite you to stop by. All of us at the brewery look forward to welcoming you! Business Hours:9:00 AM – 6:00 PMOpen year-round (shortened hours during the New Year holiday)Tasting: Free (approximately 5 varieties available at all times)Brewery Tours: Reservation by phone required (available only when a guide is on staff. breweries.name: Maihime
breweries.address: 2-9-25, Suwa, Suwa Shi, Nagano Ken, 392-0004, Japan
breweries.description: Along the Koshu Kaido in Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture, there is a stretch of just 500 meters where five sake breweries stand side by side.While all five breweries use the same spring water from Kirigamine as their brewing water, each has developed its own distinctive and unique sake, earning them the collective name Suwa Gokura (The Five Breweries of Suwa).One of these breweries, Maihime, began brewing sake in Suwa in 1894. Since then, we have embraced the motto "Visible Faces, Visible Brewing," combining tradition and innovation in our sake-making.We invite you to experience the dedication and craftsmanship that goes into every bottle of Maihime sake. Business Hours:9:30 AM – 5:00 PMTasting Hours:9:30 AM – 4:30 PM (paid tasting)

breweries.name: Tsuchiya Shuzouten
breweries.address: 1914-2, Nakagomi, Saku Shi, Nagano Ken, 385-0051, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1900 (Meiji 33). Located in Shinshu Saku, the brewery uses the underground water of the Chikuma River and brews sake by hand. In particular, the ginjo sake, which was released ahead of others in the prefecture in the 1960s (Showa 40s), continues to inherit traditional brewing methods. In recent years, the brewery has also gained popularity with its "Akane Sasu," a sake made from rice cultivated without relying on pesticides in the city of Saku. breweries.name: Tanakaya Shuzouten
breweries.address: 2227, Iiyama, Iiyama Shi, Nagano Ken, 389-2253, Japan
breweries.description: In the northernmost part of Nagano Prefecture, in a heavy snowfall area called Okushinano, Based on the idea of "making something that can only be made here", we are particular about the water of the land and the rice of the land, and together with the chief sake maker and brewers who live in this area, we continue to make "local sake" packed with the climate.The basis of our sake brewing is to constantly improve and evolve while following tradition and making good sake as a matter of course. We hope that there will be a smile on the faces of those who drink "Mizuo" in the future, and we are sincerely facing sake brewing. breweries.name: Kiuchi Jouzou
breweries.address: 985, Osawa, Saku Shi, Nagano Ken, 385-0045, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1855 (Ansei 2) during the late Edo period, our brewery operates with the motto, "Everyday sake should be of the highest quality." We strive to redefine Japanese sake by leveraging the natural environment and climate of the Saku region, including innovative techniques such as frozen rice brewing.In addition to sake, we are also deeply committed to shochu production, which is cherished primarily by local restaurants. Our limited-edition products, such as amazake and sake lees, have also gained enduring popularity among customers. Brewery tours require advance notice and scheduling adjustments.

breweries.description: Founded in 1892 (Meiji 25), "Nanawarai" has been dedicated to crafting excellent sake in the mountainous region of Kiso since its inception. With a commitment to satisfying discerning sake enthusiasts, the brewery has always pursued sake with depth and character.The brewing philosophy of "Nanawarai" focuses on achieving the authentic richness of sake, emphasizing a balanced "umakuchi" flavor profile that transcends the simple dichotomy of sweetness and dryness. breweries.name: Fujimasa Shuzou
breweries.address: 450-1, Nebara, Fujinomiya Shi, Shizuoka Ken, 418-0101, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1866, our brewery is known as "the closest sake brewery to Mt. Fuji."Located in Asagiri Kogen at an elevation of 900 meters in Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture, we craft our sake using the pure spring water of Mt. Fuji. We produce a variety of sake, including junmai and junmai daiginjo. One of our highlights is a junmai daiginjo made with Yamadanishiki rice from Hyogo Prefecture, polished to 40% and fermented at low temperatures.Our sake, crafted with traditional techniques and the passion of a young brewmaster, offers a delicate, fruity aroma and a smooth, refined taste. breweries.name: Enshu Yamanaka Shuzou
breweries.address: 61, Yokosuka, Kakegawa Shi, Shizuoka Ken, 437-1301, Japan
breweries.description: We draw exceptionally soft water from a depth of 120 meters, sourced from the snowmelt of the Akaishi Mountains in the Southern Alps. With a flow rate of 260 liters per minute, this pristine water is maintained at a consistent temperature of 16°C throughout the year, forming the foundation of the flavor and quality of our renowned sake, Aoitenka.Rather than prioritizing efficiency, we remain committed to preserving Japan's traditional brewing methods. For steaming rice, we use traditional wagama pots instead of mechanical steamers, and for pressing, we employ the fune-shibori method using traditional fune presses rather than automated machines. These time-honored practices ensure the authenticity and craftsmanship of our sake. Business Hours:9:00 AM – 5:00 PMClosed on Sundays and public holidays (subject to change)

breweries.description: "Sake Brewed with Mt. Fuji" – TakasagoOur brewery was established in 1830 (Tenpo 1) by Shokichi Yamanaka, located on the west side of Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha Shrine. Since then, we have continued brewing sake without interruption, using the spring water from Mt. Fuji, naturally filtered over a century, combined with the traditional techniques of Noto brewers.Sake brewed with the soft spring water of Mt. Fuji is known for its gentle, smooth mouthfeel and rich umami flavor. breweries.name: Sugii Shuzou
breweries.address: 4-6-4, Koishigawacho, Fujieda Shi, Shizuoka Ken, 426-0033, Japan
breweries.description: In addition to sake (Suginishiki), they also produce mirin (Asukayama) and shochu (Saisuke).In particular, the production of mirin is the only one of its kind in Shizuoka Prefecture.They specialize in kimoto starter culture and yamahai starter culture using natural lactic acid bacteria, which is in the minority in today's sake brewing.They produce dry sake with a rich and acidic taste, based on their desire to make sake with the original flavor of sake based on traditional methods. Many of the sake qualities are suitable for warmed sake, where the complexity of the flavor comes alive. Weekdays 8:00~12:00 13:00~17:00(Sales only)

breweries.name: Takashima Shuzou
breweries.address: 354-1, Hara, Numazu Shi, Shizuoka Ken, 410-0312, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1804, the brand name "Hakuin Masamune" was named after Zenji Hakuin, the founder of the Rinzai sect of Zen Buddhism, by Tesshu Yamaoka, who was impressed by the taste of sake during the Meiji era.At Takashima Sake Brewery, we brew sake by hand and carefully with the concept of the idea that sake that suits the food culture of the region is local sake.Since 2012 we have stopped adding alcohol to all of the sake we brew and have become an all-jummai-shu (pure rice) brewery. Sales are available at the brewery's direct sales office from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm on weekdays.Brewery tours or tastings are not available.

breweries.name: Haginishiki Shuzou
breweries.address: 381, Nishiwaki, Suruga Ku, Shizuoka Shi, Shizuoka Ken, 422-8044, Japan
breweries.description: Haginishiki Shuzou is a brewery located near the sea in Shizuoka City, Shizuoka Prefecture. Founded in 1876, it is best known for its flagship brand, Haginishiki.The brewery's sake is crafted using abundant soft groundwater that naturally springs up within the brewery. This water gives Haginishiki sake a gentle, smooth character with a soft flavor profile. Designed with food pairing in mind, the sake is subtly aromatic, enhancing the unique qualities of the dishes it accompanies.Carefully brewed in small quantities with Shizuoka's renowned natural water, these sakes are perfect for enjoying alongside a meal. breweries.name: Oomuraya Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 1-1-8, Hontori, Shimada Shi, Shizuoka Ken, 427-0022, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1832 (Tenpo 3), our brewery originated as a sake producer for travelers passing through Shimada-juku, offering comfort to discerning visitors from Edo.Our goal is to create sake that shines with the unique character of a local brew. We prioritize raw material processing, using the latest rice polishing equipment to perform in-house milling. For koji production, we adhere to the philosophy of "putting in the effort and crafting sake with the senses." All of our sake, from regular offerings to daiginjo, is made using traditional 10kg koji boxes.We aim to craft sake that pairs beautifully with meals, featuring the elegant and refined aromas characteristic of Shizuoka yeast, along with a deep, full-bodied flavor profile. Unfortunately, brewery Tours and retail sales are not available.Thank you for your understanding.

breweries.name: Doi Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 633, Onuki, Kakegawa Shi, Shizuoka Ken, 437-1407, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1872 (Meiji 5), our brewery has been dedicated to crafting Japanese sake for over 150 years. We use Shizuoka yeast in all of our sake, creating flavors and aromas that cannot be replicated outside the region. The most famous strain, HD-1, was originally discovered in our fermenting mash.In the Shizuoka Prefecture Sake Appraisal, we have won the Governor’s Award (1st place) 9 times in the past 10 years, significantly outpacing the second-highest record of 6 awards. Internationally, we have also received numerous top prizes in global competitions, boasting an impressive success rate.For over 15 years, we have taken proactive steps to minimize the environmental impact of our brewery. Initiatives include installing solar power systems and a facility for treating washing water onsite.Situated in a region blessed with Japan's tallest mountain, its deepest bay, and an abundance of diverse ingredients, we are committed to crafting sake that complements the rich culinary heritage of this area. Brewery Tours:Available for individuals in the food and beverage or liquor retail industries. We welcome guests from overseas, limited to those in the same industries.Maximum group size: 10 people. (Free of charge)

breweries.address: 3-57, Tenjinmachi, Chuo Ku, Hamamatsu Shi, Shizuoka Ken, 430-0808, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1871 (Meiji 4), Hamamatsu Shuzou is located about 2 kilometers from JR Hamamatsu Station, a 30-minute walk along the old Tokaido road in the heart of the city. The brewery consists of two facilities: the Showa Brewery and the Heisei Brewery.The brewery's flagship brand, Shussejo, derives its name from Hamamatsu Castle, a local symbol also known as "Shussejo" due to its association with success and good fortune. This auspicious name has made our brewery beloved by many. breweries.name: Shizuoka Hiraki Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-1, Mariko Shinden, Suruga Ku, Shizuoka Shi, Shizuoka Ken, 421-0111, Japan
breweries.description: Shizuoka Hiraki Shuzo is a newly established brewery founded in 2012.Its origins trace back to the early Meiji period when its founder, Kihei Totsuka, began as a rice merchant under the trade name "Hiraki." Starting from a rice wholesaler, Hiraki expanded its business into sake distribution and later acquired a brewing license, transitioning into the production of sake.As Hiraki Shuzo, it produced local sake in Shizuoka until the mid-Showa era. However, the brewery eventually ceased sake production in the region and relinquished its brewing license. After more than 50 years, the brewery revived its sake production at the foot of the Abe River, known for its clear streams.Today, the brewery exclusively produces junmai sake, with an annual output of approximately 200 koku (around 36,000 liters), primarily for local consumption. breweries.name: Kamisugi Shuzou
breweries.address: 20-5, Meijihonmachi, Anjo Shi, Aichi Ken, 446-0062, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in October of Bunka 2 (1805), the brewery bears the distinguished name "Kamisugi", bestowed by the Ōmiwa Shrine (Miwa Shrine) in Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture, which the seventh-generation Sugimoto Shobei deeply revered as the guardian deity of brewing.Kamisugi is crafted using abundant, high-quality underground water and locally grown premium sake rice varieties, Wakamizu and Yumeginka, from Anjo. These ingredients are polished and brewed with traditional techniques, resulting in exceptional sake year after year.Since its founding, the brewery has remained dedicated to the pursuit of unparalleled sake-making, striving daily to create sake that is truly one of a kind. brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: We have been proudly brewing for over 250 years as the birthplace of Mikawa Mirin.Our brewery has preserved its time-honored methods and traditions throughout its long history.Our flagship product, Hon Mirin Kokonoe Sakura, has received numerous awards both domestically and internationally. Notably, it earned the prestigious Honorary Grand Prize at the National Liquor Appraisal Fair during the Taisho and early Showa eras, making it a true standout.This exceptional mirin is a favorite among renowned establishments, including Michelin-starred restaurants and top chefs across various culinary genres. It has also been cherished across generations in many households, bringing professional-grade quality to everyday cooking.While steadfastly upholding tradition, we also embrace the challenge of exploring new possibilities for mirin, striving to expand its appeal and potential.In 2018, we opened Restaurant & Cafe K-an and the direct sales shop Ishikawa Hachiroji Store in a beautifully renovated residence built by the founding family over 200 years ago.Furthermore, we are committed to offering safe, natural products, free from unnecessary additives, including innovative items made from mirin and mirin lees. ・Mirin Brewery Guided Tourhttps://kokonoe.co.jp/meet07Held three times a week on Wednesdays, Thursdays, and FridaysOne tour per day in the morning (10:00 AM)Reservation required at least three days in advance・Restaurant & Café K-Anhttps://kokonoe.co.jp/k-an・Ishikawa Hachiroji StoreTasting availablehttps://kokonoe.co.jp/directstoreClosed on Mondays and Tuesdays

breweries.description: Founded in 1844, Kunizakari is a sake brewery located in Handa City, Aichi Prefecture. The brand name Kunizakari was chosen with the hope of "prospering together with this nation."The brewery produces a wide range of products, including the sake brands Kunizakari, Handago, Saika, Gahojin, and Toraji no Uta. In addition, they craft plum wine (umeshu) and plum syrup made exclusively from plums grown in their own orchard, as well as the Fruria series of high-fruit-content liqueurs.Continuously evolving to adapt to changing times, the brewery remains dedicated to the art of sake-making while expanding its offerings to meet diverse tastes. breweries.description: Founded in 1848 (Kaei 1) in Tokoname City, Aichi Prefecture, an area blessed with excellent water quality and maritime transport, Sawada Shuzou has a history spanning over 170 years. Using pure spring water from Chita, we craft our sake with a dedication to traditional hand-brewing techniques, faithfully preserving the basics and emphasizing the rich flavors of rice.The brand name Hakuro embodies our wish for the longevity and well-being of our customers.We remain deeply rooted in the brewing culture of Chita, Aichi, and will continue to create sake that brings joy to our customers, drawing from the natural blessings of the Chita region. breweries.name: Tsurumi Shuzou
breweries.address: 46, Asahi, Hyakucho, Tsushima Shi, Aichi Ken, 496-0017, Japan
breweries.description: Tsushima, where flourished as a vital hub connecting Owari and Ise via the Kiso Three Rivers, has a rich history dating back to the Kamakura period. Its location in a water-rich area fostered industrial development, and during the Sengoku period, under Oda Nobunaga, Tsushima was known as the "Kitchen of Nobunaga," serving as a commercial center for Owari.Founded in 1873, our brewery preserves the traditional methods passed down in the Tsushima region while skillfully integrating modern quality control practices. We are committed to developing rich, full-bodied sake that fully brings out the natural umami of rice, blending the craftsmanship of handmade brewing with contemporary innovation. breweries.name: Kintora Shuzou
breweries.address: 3-11-6, Yamada, Kita Ku, Nagoya Shi, Aichi Ken, 462-0810, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1845 (Kōka 2), our brewery began its journey in what was then Yamada Village, located along the Zenkoji Highway near the castle town of Nagoya. The founder, Zenbee Osakaya, laid the foundation for our legacy.The name "Kintora" was bestowed during the early Showa period by the third-generation Zenbee. Being born in the Year of the Tiger (Tora), he combined this with inspiration from the golden shachihoko adorning Nagoya Castle. Taking the "fish" radical from the character for shachihoko, he crafted the name "Kintora" (Golden Tiger).With a commitment to crafting refined, light, and smooth sake, inspired by the Echigo brewing tradition, we take meticulous care in brewing each tank, ensuring every step is handled with dedication.In recent years, our efforts have earned us numerous accolades, including seven Gold Prizes at the Annual Japan Sake Awards, the Aichi Prefectural Governor’s Award for three consecutive years, and the Grand Gold Prize at the "Delicious Sake in a Wine Glass Awards." These honors reflect our exceptional craftsmanship, particularly in ginjo-style brewing. Sangane as our primary ingredients, we apply our unique expertise to every step of the process—from polishing the rice to fermentation, storage, and bottling.With meticulous quality control at each stage, we are relentless in our pursuit of authentic, unparalleled flavor. breweries.name: Sekiya Jouzou
breweries.address: 22, Machiura Taguchi, Shitaracho, Kitashitara Gun, Aichi Ken, 441-2301, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1864, this sake brewery is located in the Okumikawa region of Aichi Prefecture. The brewery is particular about the rice used in its sake, and all rice is milled in-house. They also cultivate their own sake rice. Additionally, the brewery manufactures its own alcohol for additives from the sake lees, making full use of raw materials and focusing on sustainability and environmental considerations. The brewery also produces its own shochu and liqueurs made from locally grown fruits. In addition to the main brewery, there is the "Houraisen Ginjo Kobo," where custom-made sake is produced, and the "Houraisen Sake Labo," where visitors can experience sake brewing. Sanage, a sacred peak in Mikawa.For over 160 years, we have honored traditional sake-brewing methods while embracing modern techniques to craft exceptional sake.Kikuishi is renowned for its gentle umami, delicate flavor, and clean, refreshing aftertaste. breweries.description: This sake brewery was newly established in 2021 by food manufacturer Imuraya (Tsu City, Mie Prefecture), which took over the sake production business of Fukui Sake Brewery Co., whic founded in 1900. The brewery is characterized by its terroir-rooted sake brewing, using pure water and high-quality sake rice nurtured in the rich climate of Mie. The brewery is also committed to brewing sake in all four seasons, delivering freshly brewed, delicious sake at any time of the year. The symbol mark is a superposition of "rice (*)," which is the main ingredient of sake, and "I (well)," which is one of the letters in the company names of both companies and means a place where people gather. breweries.description: In 1853, the era was in turmoil due to the arrival of the Black Ships.Gizaemon, the seventh generation of the Shigeto family, received a sealed document from the Todo clan and began brewing sake in Iga Aoyama. The clear air of Aoyama with its deep greenery. Polished water flowing from a mountain stream and high-quality Iga rice.And the harshness of the cold winter.Surrounded by the blessings that are indispensable for sake brewing, we have continued to brew sake with the utmost attention to quality first. breweries.address: 1387-1, Katsuno, Takashima Shi, Shiga Ken, 520-1121, Japan
breweries.description: Since founded in the Kan'en era in the middle of the Edo period (1603-1868), we have continued our sake brewing in Katsuno, Takashima-cho, in the Omi region for over 270 years. The name "Haginotsuyu" was given by the feudal lord at the time of the founding of the company in reference to the bush clovers that grow along the shores of Lake Biwa. Using high quality Omi sake rice and water from the Mt.Hira range (sweet and soft water), we aim to create a mellow, delicious sake with a clean aftertaste that is easy to appreciate the more you drink it, the philosophy is "Sake to savor, not to get drunk". Weekdays 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.Tours of the brewery are not available, but sake tasting and shopping are available.

breweries.address: 83, Asahi, Shinasahicho, Takashima Shi, Shiga Ken, 520-1501, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1865 (the first year of Keio). Kawashima Sake Brewery has a brewery in the famous water place "Kabata" around Lake Biwa. The brewery, which is walking only in the line of "Matsu-no-hana", a famous sake with an unparalleled taste brewed by Omi rice cultivated from clean water and fertile soil, has started the Lake Biwa Distillery with the aim of becoming a world-class Japanese whisky by taking advantage of the strong water and the land of Takashima, the hometown of fermentation.This is a unique opportunity for a Japan sake brewery to also work on a distillery. We look forward to your visit. breweries.name: Emishiki Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-7-8, Minakuchicho Honmachi, Koka Shi, Shiga Ken, 528-0031, Japan
breweries.description: Our company was founded in 1892 (Meiji 25) by Eizaburo Takeshima under the name Takeshima Honke. It began brewing sake at its current location in Koka City, Shiga Prefecture, in the heart of Minakuchi-juku, a post town along the old Tokaido road.Our flagship trademark, Emishiki, was named by the second-generation owner, Senjiro Takeshima. The name embodies the philosophy that “sake enriches life and brings people closer together. May we enjoy laughter and sake in every season.” It also reflects our dedication to crafting sake so exquisite that it brings a spontaneous smile to those who taste it.

breweries.description: Village Koka [Hidden Japan Sake Famous Production Area] Koka, where Tanaka Sake Brewery is located, is known as Ninja Village. Founded in 1911, the fifth-generation owner brews the famous sake "Harunomine" by himself. All rice is local Koga sake rice "Tamae" and "Yamada Nishiki" and the water is our own groundwater (Suzuka's underground water) and is weakly soft water.The origin of the name "Harunomine" comes from the fact that the sake is brewed in the extreme cold when the mountains of Suzuka are visible to the east and the soft spring air can be seen on one of the peaks. breweries.description: The brewery is located on the shore of Lake Biwa.Kaizu, Makino Town, Takashima City, has a lot of snow in winter, but it never freezes on the shore of the large lake.In summer, the cool breezes from the lake gently protect the brewery.The sake's name, "Chikubushima", comes from an island in Lake Biwa.It is believed by many to be an island where a god resides, and in recent years it has become a popular power spot.With the help of such a god, ”Chikubushima” continues its sake brewing. breweries.name: Saitou Shuzou
breweries.address: 105, Yokooji Misuyamashiro Yashikicho, Fushimi Ku, Kyoto Shi, Kyoto Fu, 612-8207, Japan
breweries.description: The Saito family started the sake brewing business in the 28th year of the Meiji era. The Saito family had been running a kimono business in Fushimi since the Genroku period, but in the Meiji era, the ninth generation Sotaro Saito changed his business from a kimono merchant to a sake brewing business amid major changes in society.At the time of its founding, it was sold under trademarks such as "Masamune Yanagi" and "Otaka", but in commemoration of the enthronement of Emperor Taisho in the 4th year of the Taisho era, it was changed to the current representative trademark "Eikun". At this time, in memory of the ninth generation Sotaro, who had already passed away, the name was given including "Isao" from the commandment.After that, we overcame the difficult period during and after the war, and became a corporation (joint-stock company) in Showa 35, which continues to this day. All of our sake is junmai, made with care and dedication.The brand Soku was chosen with the hope that, just as looking up at a clear blue sky brings a sense of peace, those who drink our sake will feel a gentle warmth in their hearts. breweries.name: Hakurei Shuzou
breweries.address: 949, Yura, Miyazu Shi, Kyoto Fu, 626-0071, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1832 in Tango Yura, Miyazu City, Kyoto Prefecture, this is a traditional sake brewery that has continued since the Edo period. We produce many award-winning sake and liqueurs. Although the water is extremely soft with a hardness of 11.9 mg/L, it is a mysterious water that can be used to freely brew both sweet and dry sake, and is the origin of sake brewing. Sake is brewed using carefully selected ingredients and is made by brewers using traditional brewing methods.We continue to inherit the brewing methods that have been passed down since our founding. If the direct sales store is open during business hours, you can sample almost all types.Brewery tours are available from Thursday to Sunday from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. Reservations must be made the day before.

breweries.description: Fushimi, Kyoto, where our Fushimi factory is located, is the birthplace of Takara Shuzo and has been called "Fushimizu" since ancient times, a sake brewing area in Kyoto with good quality underground water suitable for sake brewing. Today, Takara Shuzo's Fushimi factory has two locations, one in the east and the other in the west.The plant produces sake, mirin, and chu-hai, and has 12 different bottling lines, making it Takara Shuzo's main plant for supplying products to the western Japan region, we are further strengthening our commitment to safety and security in response to diversifying customer needs. breweries.name: Kinoshita Shuzou
breweries.address: 1512, Kumihamacho Koyama, Kyotango Shi, Kyoto Fu, 629-3442, Japan
breweries.description: We have inherited the "Tamagawa" sake brewing since 1842 (Tempo 13th year of the Emperor's reign). Currently, Englishman Philip Harper is the toji (a chief sake maker), and his abundance of imagination and challenging sake brewing techniques have created new fans. In particular, the "Natural Brewing" series, brewed with the power of microorganisms living in the brewery without the addition of pure cultured yeast or lactic acid, produces sake that can be said to be the foundation and character of Tamagawa. Tamagawa's main theme is "maturity" and we cherish the mystical process of transforming sake into matured sake from the moment it is born as "Shiboritate" and work hard every day to brew sake that will give our customers a deep emotional experience through all five senses and time axis. The sales store attached to the brewery is open from 9:00 to 17:00 on weekends and holidays (except January 1).Visitors can enjoy tasting and shopping for regular, seasonal, and brewery-exclusive sake.If you visit the store in a group of 10 or more people, please make a reservation in advance (through the website or by phone).

breweries.name: Gekkeikan
breweries.address: 247, Minamihamacho, Fushimi Ku, Kyoto Shi, Kyoto Fu, 612-8660, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1637, Gekkeikan is the oldest sake brewery in Fushimi, Kyoto.In the Meiji era (1868-1912), Gekkeikan established the first research institute for sake brewers, introduced science and technology to sake brewing, brought innovation to brewing technology, and expanded its business.Today, under the corporate concept of "Aiming for Health, Sake Science, and Creating Pleasure", Gekkeikan produces high-quality sake in all genres, from high-end to popular, and exports sake not only domestically but also internationally, thereby expanding the sake culture globally. Gekkeikan Okura Memorial Museum, a sake archive, is operated.This facility allows visitors to experience the history of the development of sake brewing in Fushimi, Kyoto and Gekkeikan's challenging and creative spirits.After the tour, please enjoy tasting three types of sake from a selection of ten types prepared according to the season.

breweries.description: Founded in 1675, Tokubee Masuda Shoten is one of the oldest existing sake breweries in Fushimi, Kyoto. The company produces sake under the name "Tsuki no Katsura.Sake brewing is not possible without human sensibility.The brewery has always been committed to handcrafting sake and has focused on the relationship between sake and culture in a wide range of areas, including sake containers, cuisine, traditional events, and the arts, as well as sake itself, and has always valued sake as an enjoyable drink.As a sake brewery that has been producing unique sake such as nigori sake and aged sake, we aim to produce unconventional sake that is not influenced by the world, while preserving tradition. breweries.name: Shirasugi Shuzou
breweries.address: 954, Omiyacho Suki, Kyotango Shi, Kyoto Fu, 629-2503, Japan
breweries.description: Shirasugi Shuzou is located in Kyotango City, the northernmost region of Kyoto, and has been crafting sake for over 240 years since its founding in 1777.Our sake is made by a small team of six, including the brewmaster and president, Satoru Shirasugi, with an annual production of approximately 300 koku.We exclusively use delicious table rice grown locally in Tango, one of Japan’s premier rice-producing regions, and do not use any specialized sake-brewing rice such as Yamada Nishiki.Recently, we have been exploring new horizons in sake brewing, creating sweet and tart sake using black and white koji, and experimenting with innovative methods that have never been tried before. breweries.description: A sake brewery founded in 1847 during the Edo period, located in Hachiei-cho, Kyotango City, northern Kyoto Prefecture.After closing down during the war, four local sake breweries joined forces to form Takeno Sake Brewery Co.,.Known for its brand name "Yasakatsuru", the company cooperates with local rice farmers to produce and sell sake made from locally grown rice.The company focuses on brewing sake that matches the characteristics of the rice and the quality of fresh sake, and continues to create unique sake that is not bound by conventional methods, including the development of a special sake that incorporates an unprecedented production method by Yoshiki Yukimachi chief sake maker. 9:00 - 17:00 on brewery business days(Reservations priority, up to 10 people, call or email in advance to make a reservation) (We generally do not accept brewery tours.)

The taste is elegant and delicate taste brewed with "Kyo-no-mizu" of the place of scenic beauty.It has been nurtured by the culture of Kyoto and has enhanced Kyoto cuisine.Currently, we are brewing our sake in Fushimi, Kyoto, using the famous "Tokiwaisui" water, and are striving for a new history while preserving the tradition. breweries.name: Tamanohikari Shuzou
breweries.address: 545-2, Higashisakaimachi, Fushimi Ku, Kyoto Shi, Kyoto Fu, 612-8066, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1673 (Enpo 1), Tamanohikari is a junmai ginjo kura that crafts sake using only rice, koji, and water. We are dedicated to the finest ingredients, and in 2022, we obtained both domestic and international organic certifications, highlighting our commitment to producing organic sake.Our sake-making process begins with rice polishing, followed by steaming in traditional koshiki steamers, and fully handcrafted koji production. We emphasize handcrafting wherever possible, preserving the traditions of sake brewing.Our goal is to create “honest sake.” Tamanohikari is known for its flavors that complement cuisine and offer a taste you can enjoy time and time again. breweries.description: Towa Sake Brewery was founded in 1717 near Ikunojuku on the Kyoto Highway. Today, it is the only sake brewery in Fukuchiyama City that produces sake under the brand names "Fukuchi Sanman Nisengoku" and "Rokkan".Although the brewery has a long history, it is a revived brewery that resumed its own brewing in 2011 after a 33-year hiatus since 1977.Currently, the brewer's eldest daughter serves as the Tamba chief sake maker, and the brewery's employees make small quantities of sake by hand. breweries.address: 7-11-2, Kisabe, Katano Shi, Osaka Fu, 576-0052, Japan
breweries.description: Located in the Katano region, where Osaka, Kyoto, and Nara meet, our brewery has been passed down through generations since the late Edo period. As a small-scale brewery producing around 500 koku annually, approximately 90% of our production consists of premium sake (Tokutei Meisho-shu), with 40% of that being undiluted sake (genshu), reflecting our strong focus on genshu.Using the abundant spring water from the Ikoma mountain range and high-quality sake rice, we work with outstanding teamwork to ensure the best quality in every step of the process—from brewing to storage, bottling, product management, and shipping—so that we deliver sake in its optimal condition to our customers. breweries.description: Amano-shu is a sake brewery that has its origins in the monk's house sake Amano-shu, which is known to have been a favorite drink of the warlord Hideyoshi Taikoh.Founded in 1718, the brewery aims to produce sake with a full-bodied sweet and savory flavor, rather than a dry, all-out sake.Since 2001, the brewery has won the gold medal 13 times at the National New Sake Competition. *Opening hours are 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. on weekdays, weekends, and holidays. breweries.description: Founded in 1921, Izumisano City's only sake brewery is located in Hinenoso, a Japanese Heritage certified area on the other side of Kansai Airport, where the rich natural landscapeFounded in 1921, this is the only sake brewery in Izumisano City, located in the culturally rich, Japan Heritage-designated area of Hine-no-sho, near Kansai Airport and surrounded by traditional, scenic landscapes.With a dedication to “quality over quantity,” our team of five employees handles every stage of production by hand, honoring traditional methods. This commitment has been recognized with numerous awards, including gold and merit prizes at the Annual Japan Sake Awards and the Seishu Appraisal Awards.Our brewery’s direct sales shop, open all day, offers sake for purchase and tasting. Additionally, our Kuramoto Cafe, open on weekends and holidays, serves lunches, sweets, and drinks featuring ingredients unique to our brewery. of olden times is still preserved.The brewery has a direct sales shop and ”Kura Moto Cafe”, and also offers tours of the brewery and an official online store. breweries.name: Sawa no Tsuru
breweries.address: 5-1-2, Shinzaike Minamimachi, Nada Ku, Kobe Shi, Hyogo Ken, 657-0864, Japan
breweries.description: The company was founded in 1717. The origin of the company is designated as the first generation who ran a rice shop and started brewing sake in Nishigo, Nada as a sideline business.The "*" mark, which is now one of Sawanotsuru's marks, is taken from the character for rice.It is from this origin that Sawanotsuru is dedicated to rice and to sake made only from rice, junmai-shu.Together with "rice" (Yamadanishiki), "water" (Miyamizu), and "people (Tamba chief sake maker)", we aim to be the best of quality of sake in Japan as "Nada Honryu no Sake". Tours and tastings are available at the Sawanotsuru Museum.For more information, please visit the official website.

breweries.name: Kikumasamune Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-7-15, Mikagehommachi, Higashinada Ku, Kobe Shi, Hyogo Ken, 658-0046, Japan
breweries.description: It was founded in 1659, during the reign of the fourth shogun, Ietsuna.In line with the development of Edo (Tokyo), Nada became popular as "Kudarizake (sake sent from Nada to Edo)" and developed greatly during the Bunka-Bunsei period of the late Edo period (1603-1868).Under the vision of "Tradition and Innovation", Nada Brewery continues to brew sake with a "quality-oriented" approach, focusing on ingredients and the skills of the chief sake makers, while maintaining the "kimoto starter culture" of sake brewing that has been a tradition of Nada since the Edo period.We use Miyamizu, the mysterious and famous water from the Rokko mountain range, and Yamada-Nishiki, the highest quality sake rice in Hyogo Prefecture, with the utmost care. Open year-round (except year-end and New Year holidays).Open 9:30-16:30 (admission until 16:00).Reservations required for groups of 10 or more (free of charge).Please check availability and make reservations online.Paid and free tasting available.

breweries.name: Daiwa Shuzou
breweries.address: 1063-2, Nishijima, Okubocho, Akashi Shi, Hyogo Ken, 674-0065, Japan
breweries.description: After producing sake in China until the end of World War II, Daiwa Shuzou was established in Akashi in 1955 (Showa 30).Under the leadership of the late Noboru Yabuta, who served as managing director at the time, the company developed the "Continuous Moromi Pressing Machine," a device capable of quickly separating moromi into sake and sake lees.By blending traditional sake brewing techniques with state-of-the-art production equipment, Daiwa Shuzou has consistently maintained the same flavor and quality, continuing to craft exceptional sake.

breweries.name: Kokusan Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-12-34, Higashimachi, Nishinomiya Shi, Hyogo Ken, 662-0922, Japan
breweries.description: The company was founded in 1861 and has a history of about 160 years. Before the war, it was known as the Kakushin Heijuro Shoten, and 10 breweries, mainly in its current location, recorded a maximum sake brewing volume of about 10,000 goku (1,800 kl.), but most of it was destroyed by fire in World War II. In Showa 23, the company adopted the "Kokusan" seal, which had been mainly used for a long time, and changed the organization to the current company name, and inherited the name as Daiginjo sake "Kokusanichi". In Showa 35, "Nada Jiman" was released, and it was named as a sake that can be proud of in the main production area Nada Gogo. Carefully selected sake rice is polished to a high level of refinement and prepared with the famous water "Miyamizu" given by heaven, and it is finished in a rich and flavorful "sake of autumn glory". Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.description: Since its founding, Hakutsuru Shuzou has remained dedicated to the art of sake-making. Through the brewing and sales of sake, we have supported and enriched the culinary and cultural aspects of people’s lives.With pride as inheritors of a rich sake-brewing tradition, we are committed to continuing business activities that contribute to and enhance daily life. The Hakutsuru Sake Brewery Museum, located on the premises, was established in the remodeled Head Office Ichigo-gura, which was actually used until the 1960s.The museum is open from 9:30 to 16:30 (admission until 16:00. breweries.description: Founded in 1716, it is the oldest sake brewery in the Tamba region. We brew local sake that makes the most of the individuality of each of them, including the traditional techniques of Mr. Tamba chief sake maker that have been passed down since ancient times, locally grown sake rice, the Mt. Jinchiji underground water that springs from the brewery, and the various microorganisms that have settled in the brewery over a history of more than 300 years.We aim for a gentle and gentle taste that you will not get tired of drinking as a late drink. Tastings are not available.Please make a reservation at least 10 days prior to the desired date.However, we cannot accept tours during the peak season (December).

breweries.name: Eigashima Shuzou
breweries.address: 919, Nishijima, Okubocho, Akashi Shi, Hyogo Ken, 674-0065, Japan
breweries.description: Eigashima Sake Brewery is located in Akashi City, Hyogo Prefecture, overlooking the Seto Inland Sea.Founded in 1888 with the support of five breweries in the area, the brewery has since pursued forward-thinking initiatives, such as being the first in Japan to sell sake in one square bottle, exporting to Hawaii in 1899, and obtaining a license to produce whiskey and other Western-style liquors in 1919.Currently, we have adopted the "mizu-hashiroshi brewing" method, a reproduction of Muromachi Period sake brewing, and are constantly researching to deliver sake that will remain in the hearts of those who drink it.In addition to representative brand "Kamitaka," the limited edition "Yamatodamashii" is a unique sake that is gaining more and more fans. Tours: Held irregularly (about once a week, by appointment only)You can visit the sake brewery and the whiskey distillery.Please check https://www.ei-sake.jp/tours.html for registration and more information.

The Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake that occurred in 1995 caused extensive damage, but the following year, in 1996, the brewery was completely renovated into a brewery where you can observe the sake brewing process through glass, and as a tour sake brewery that combines "making", "showing" and "selling", it is visited by tourists from various regions both in Japan and overseas. 10:00-17:00 (Closed on Mondays, but if Monday is a national holiday, the next day is closed.For groups of 10 or more, a guide will be provided upon advance reservation.Basically, you are free to visit the museum (no reservation required).

breweries.name: Honda Shouten
breweries.address: 361-1, Takata, Aboshiku, Himeji Shi, Hyogo Ken, 671-1226, Japan
breweries.description: With the basic philosophy of "rice sake tastes like rice", the company brews sake exclusively with Yamada-Nishiki produced in Hyogo Prefecture's Special A district and other rice suitable for sake brewing.We were one of the first in the industry to brew daiginjo, which has won gold medals a total of 26 times at the National New Sake Competition.In addition, in order to create new values for sake, we are taking on various challenges, such as contract farming with benevolent farmers for the first time in Japan, research on terroir, revival of sake rice, efforts to produce aged sake, and joint research with industry and academia. Tastings and purchases are available at the Terroir Pavilion, which is located in the company from 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. on weekdays.

breweries.description: Founded in 1900, King Brewery was established in Inami-cho, one of the best grain-growing areas in Hyogo Prefecture.Even today, our company continues to produce and sell condiment of "mirin (sweet cooking sake)" and "sake (cooking sake)", both of which are familiar to consumers under the Hinode brand name, as well as sake under the "Banshu-Nishiki" and "Inami brands".The "Inamicho-grown series", which is made using locally grown produce and the simplest possible production method, includes "Rokujo no Shizuku", an honkaku shochu made from Rokujo barley, a specialty of Inamicho.

breweries.description: Our company was founded in 1874 in Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture.The name of our flagship brand, Seiten, is derived from the Meiji Emperor's Grand Enthronement Ceremony, which took place around the time of our founding. Brewed with the subterranean water of the largest river in Hyogo, the Kakogawa River, and locally grown sake rice from Hyogo Prefecture, Seiten is produced with a commitment to traditional handcrafted brewing methods. As the only long-established sake brewery in Kakogawa City, we continue to create sake that is deeply rooted in our local community. breweries.name: Tanaka Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 3-583, Hirohataku Hommachi, Himeji Shi, Hyogo Ken, 671-1114, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1835, the rich history of Tanaka Shuzoujo continues to this day under the stewardship of its sixth-generation owner. In the past, the sake brewed by this brewery delighted the lords of Himeji Castle. To honor this legacy, the brewery’s flagship brand, made with premium Yamadanishiki rice from Hyogo Prefecture, bears the name of Himeji Castle’s moniker, "Shirasagi no Shiro (White Egret Castle)."Tanaka Shuzoujo’s motto is “Onko Chishin”—respecting the wisdom of the past while creating new value. This reflects their passion for offering customers more than just sake to enjoy, but also the opportunity to connect with the history and stories behind each product. breweries.name: Ibaraki Shuzou

breweries.description: Founded in 1848, with a legacy spanning 170 years, the 9th-generation brewmaster crafts unique sake and amazake exclusively under the "Rairaku" brand.Every step of the process is meticulously handled by the brewmaster and the brewery team, ensuring a handcrafted touch throughout. From washing the rice, making koji, and managing fermentation to bottling and labeling, each small batch is produced with care and precision. This human-centered approach results in high-quality sake that you can enjoy with confidence.Not mass-produced, these rare and historically rich sake offerings make the perfect gift for sake enthusiasts. breweries.name: Kenbishi Shuzou
breweries.address: 3-12-5, Mikagehommachi, Higashinada Ku, Kobe Shi, Hyogo Ken, 658-0046, Japan
breweries.description: Since founded in the 2nd year of Eisho (1505), Kenbishi has continued to be made for more than 500 years, shifting the stage of brewing from Itami to Nada. With the family motto, "Be a stopped watch!", I am devoted to protecting the taste I believe in without being swept away by trends. To that end, we spare no effort in investing in raw materials and handcrafting, and we are working hard to make careful sake using the traditional manufacturing method.Please enjoy once Kenbishi's sake, which walks with customers with the "determination not to change the taste". Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.name: Tatsuuma Honke Shuzou
breweries.address: 2-10, Tateishocho, Nishinomiya Shi, Hyogo Ken, 662-0943, Japan
breweries.description: For more than 360 years since its founded in 1662, we have been brewing sake in Nishinomiya, Hyogo Prefecture, supported by the rich natural blessings of miyamizu (miya water), high-quality rice, and the Rokko downpour.With the belief that "sake is not something to be made, but something to be nurtured", we will continue to work together with the local community, while preserving the essence of Shiroshika's sake brewing, adopting new techniques, making further changes for the sake brewing that will continue into the future, and making efforts in educational programs to foster people and social contributions that support the local culture. breweries.name: Koube Shushinkan
breweries.address: 1-8-17, Mikagetsukamachi, Higashinada Ku, Kobe Shi, Hyogo Ken, 658-0044, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1751, the sake brewery making "Fukuju" has been dedicated to handcrafted sake production for 13 generations in Mikage-go."Fukuju Junmai Ginjo" has gained worldwide recognition, having been served at official Nobel Prize events. In recent years, the brewery has embraced the United Nations' SDGs, implementing "sustainable sake brewing" practices. These efforts have been internationally acclaimed, earning the brewery an award from an environmental organization overseas.The brewery's grounds feature four integrated facilities: the brewing hall "Fukuju-Gura," the retail shop "Tomei-Gura," the brewery restaurant "Sakabayashi," and the event space "Shushinkan Hall." Visitors can also enjoy a Japanese garden that showcases seasonal flowers, making it a highly recommended destination for guests from around the globe. breweries.description: Founded in 1725, all of our sake is brewed in the Tajima-style "kimototer culture" and "yamahai starter culture" with the aim of creating delicious sake brewing that goes well with local foods such as Matsuba crab, seafood, and Tajima beef.Using local hight quality sake rice from Hyogo Prefecture, Kozutsumitsuru's unique brewing process produces a sake with a robust flavor, a refreshing aroma, and a smooth taste that will keep you coming back for more triple beat. breweries.name: Sanyouhai Shuzou
breweries.address: 28, Yamasakicho Yamasaki, Shiso Shi, Hyogo Ken, 671-2577, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1837. Located in the mid-western part of Hyogo Prefecture, about 100 km from Kobe City and about 30 km from Himeji City, and bordered by Okayama Prefecture to the west and Tottori Prefecture to the north, we make the Japan sake "Banshu Ikkon" in a mountainous area bordering Okayama Prefecture to the west. The brand name means "Please have a cup of our sake packed with the charm of Banshu". Using sake rice from Hyogo Prefecture, the best place for sake rice in Japan, and the pure water of the Hyonosen Mountains, we deliver a gem brewed by taking advantage of the climate with temperature differences suitable for sake making that snows in winter. Brewery tours are not available.Partial tasting available (may not be available depending on public conditions and crowds).At the "Banshu Ikkon Store" located next to the restaurant, visitors can purchase "Banshu Ikkon", "CIDRE RonRon" made with apples from Hyogo Prefecture, and other specialty products of Shiso City.

breweries.name: Sakura Masamune
breweries.address: 5-10-1, Uozaki Minamimachi, Higashinada Ku, Kobe Shi, Hyogo Ken, 658-0025, Japan
breweries.description: Sakura Masamune is a sake brewery founded in 1625.The brewery is proud of its history and traditions, including the original brewery that named its sake "Masamune" for the first time, the brewery that discovered "Miyamizu" which contributed greatly to the development of Nada sake, and the brewery that originated "Association No. 1 yeast" which was distributed as the first official yeast in the country, with the motto of quality first, we are working hard every day to brew sake."Sakura Masamune Yakimare Kiippon Junmai" of a regular sake, it is a junmai-shu with a gentle rice flavor and excellent sharpness, made entirely from Hyogo Prefecture's Yamadanishiki rice, which is a rice suitable for sake brewing. The Sakura Masamune Memorial Hall "Sakura Banquet" has various exhibits such as valuable old-time sake brewing videos and sake brewing tools.Free and paid tastings are also available at the store in Sakura Banquet (11:00-19:00).
breweries.visiting_info: The Sakura Masamune Memorial Hall "Sakura Banquet" has various exhibits such as valuable old-time sake brewing videos and sake brewing tools.Free and paid tastings are also available at the store in Sakura Banquet (11:00-19:00).

breweries.name: Hakutaka
breweries.address: 1-1, Hamacho, Nishinomiya Shi, Hyogo Ken, 662-0942, Japan
breweries.description: Tatsuma Etsuzo founded Hakutaka in 1862 at the present location in Nishinomiya.Using miyamizu which is water from eight wells owned by the company as brewing water, Hakutaka has inherited the traditional technique of Nada-shu, or Nada sake, and has maintained a 100% home-brewed brand without blending even a drop of sake from other makers since its establishment.Since 1924 (Taisho 13), it has been the only sake brewery in Japan to be selected as the Imperial Sake of Ise Jingu Shrine, and has been offered to the deities of the shrine without fail every single day since then. Hakutaka Rokusuien Hours: 11:00~19:00Closed on the 1st and 3rd Wednesday of the monthSat., Sun. and holidays 12:00~17:00You can enjoy Hakutaka's sake at "Kura BAR".

breweries.description: With the motto "Bringing Sake, Laughter, and Happiness to Everyone," we craft our Japanese sake Akashi-Tai using Hyogo-grown rice and traditional handmade methods. Exported to 30 countries worldwide, primarily in Europe and the Americas, AKASHI-TAI enjoys popularity abroad as well.We also use locally sourced ingredients from Hyogo Prefecture to produce liqueurs and Japanese craft gin. While preserving tradition, we are actively engaged in creating new value through innovative sake-making. breweries.name: Okutou Shouji
breweries.address: 1419-1, Sakoshi, Ako Shi, Hyogo Ken, 678-0172, Japan
breweries.description: Okutoujou was founded in 1601.It is a historical sake brewery that also served as an official liquor store for the Ako clan during the Edo period.The brewery still retains its old, relaxed atmosphere, centered around the sake brewery.Blessed with the clear water of the Chikusa River and the rich climate of the rice-producing region of Banshu, a grain-producing region, the brewery has maintained its small size as it was in those days.The brewery is a semi-underground building built on a stone wall that takes advantage of the topography. We believe it is important to continue to produce sake of satisfactory quality, at least in terms of stone height. Tasting is available at the direct sales counter.The Okufuji Sake Brewery Museum on the premises exhibits old materials on Sakagoe, a port town that prospered through maritime trade.Open Monday-Saturday9:00-17:00Please inquire during the year-end and New Year holidays and on Sundays and national holidays.Admission free

breweries.name: Nadagiku Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-121, Tegara, Himeji Shi, Hyogo Ken, 670-0972, Japan
breweries.description: In 1910, Kawaishi Sake Brewery was established by Kawaishi Shuzosaku I as an independent company from Kawaishi Honke Sake Brewery Co. Ltd. was founded in 1910, and began sake production at its current location (Tegara, Himeji City), celebrating its 100th anniversary in 2010. The company name "Nada Kiku" is derived from the combination of "Nada" of "Harimanada", the sea spreading out in front of Himeji, and "Kiku", a flower.Although our production volume is currently limited due to our small brewing and small-lot management, we believe that by carefully and painstakingly brewing local sake rice and thoroughly controlling quality, we are able to "deliver the most delicious sake in season" to our customers.We invite you to visit our brewery and try our "seasonal sake".With the motto of "Harmony of Sake and Food Culture" and the spirit of hospitality, all of us at the brewery look forward to serving you. The direct sales office and tour course are open all year round, except for the year-end and New Year's holidays.Business hours are 10:00-17:00.Tours are free of charge.Tours are free of charge.Please ask the staff at the direct sales counter for a tasting!

breweries.name: Ohsawa Honke Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-13-28, Higashimachi, Nishinomiya Shi, Hyogo Ken, 662-0922, Japan
breweries.description: Ohsawa Honke Shuzou was established in 1770 (Meiwa 7) during the mid-Edo period in Sakai, and relocated to its current location in Nada, Nishinomiya, in 1954 (Showa 29).With over 250 years of tradition and history, this brewery has passed down its handcrafted sake-making techniques from generation to generation. Adhering to the principles of traditional sake craftsmanship, the brewery employs methods such as steaming rice in a traditional iron pot and using wooden boxes for koji cultivation in a koji room. Despite the advancements in automation, Ohsawa Honke continues to embrace traditional methods, crafting sake with the same care and devotion as raising a child, even in the harsh cold of winter.To ensure every batch maintains the highest quality, the brewery limits its production volume so that the brewmaster can oversee every step of the process. Their commitment is to produce premium, flavorful sake with meticulous attention to detail. Additionally, their sales approach focuses on direct-to-consumer distribution, delivering freshly brewed sake directly from the brewery to your home. breweries.name: Karibaichi Shuzou
breweries.address: 500, Hagano, Tanba-Sasayama Shi, Hyogo Ken, 669-2103, Japan
breweries.description: Our brewery was founded by the first-generation brewmaster, a renowned Tamba Toji who had traveled across Japan mentoring others in the art of sake brewing. He chose to establish the brewery in his hometown of Tamba Sasayama, where his roots lie. Using soft water for the brewing process, our signature sake, Shugetsu, is characterized by its gentle, smooth, and approachable taste. We produce a diverse range of sake, from freshly brewed namazake to long-aged sake, offering a variety of flavors to suit different preferences. We invite you to explore and discover the sake that best matches your taste. breweries.name: Konishi Shuzou
breweries.address: 2-13, Higashiarioka, Itami Shi, Hyogo Ken, 664-0845, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1550 in Itami, the birthplace of sake. The first generation of the Konishi family started sake brewing while working in the medicine business.While carrying a barrel of sake on the back of a horse to Edo, the second generation Muneie was impressed by the nobility of the snow on the top of Mt. Fuji, he named "shirayuki".Itami has long been at the intersection of the Saigoku Kaido and Arima Roads, and sake brewing has flourished in the area due to the climate and the high-quality subsoil water from the Rokko Nagao Mountains.Clear sake from nigorizake is born in this area,also "Itami Morohaku", made with rice that was sparingly polished for both koji rice and kake rice, became a well-known sake in Edo (present-day Tokyo)."Itami Morohaku" has been recognized as a Japanese Heritage as a story of the Edo period. Although the brewery is not open to the public, visitors are free to tour the second floor of the Hakusetsu Brewery Village Longevity Storehouse, a renovated Edo Period sake brewery, where sake brewing tools are on display.
breweries.visiting_info: Although the brewery is not open to the public, visitors are free to tour the second floor of the Hakusetsu Brewery Village Longevity Storehouse, a renovated Edo Period sake brewery, where sake brewing tools are on display.

breweries.name: Yasufuku Matashirou Shouten
breweries.address: 1-5-23, Mikagetsukamachi, Higashinada Ku, Kobe Shi, Hyogo Ken, 658-0044, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1751, this sake brewery operates in Mikage, one of the Nada Gogo regions of Kobe, where it produces handcrafted sake in small batches.The flagship brand, "Daikoku Masamune," takes its name from Daikokuten, the god of wealth and prosperity, and has focused on crafting sake that enhances and complements meals, embodying the concept of "food-friendly sake."Committed to using Hyogo-grown sake rice, including the renowned "Yamadanishiki," and the famous brewing water of Nada, "Miyamizu," the brewery continues to produce local sake of Nada that fully leverages these exceptional local resources.Whether it's sake or non-alcoholic beverages, the brewery's mission remains steadfast: to "enhance the dining experience." In 2023, under the banner of "Brewing for Food," it is contributing to a richer food culture through both its sake brewing and non-alcoholic beverage businesses. Business Hours:10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (with a 1-hour lunch break)Closed:WednesdaysTasting:Tastings available at the store. breweries.description: In the sixth year of Kanbun (1666), the mysterious sake was born by Hasegawa Eiga, a descendant of the 33rd generation of Fujiwara Kamashi of the Nara Dynasty. With the old-fashioned cold brewing, we have cultivated the technique of sake brewing many times without neglecting a single day, just like the name of Yaegaki. Sake brewing has not changed for more than 350 years. The spirit of the brewery, which is devoted to handmade craftsmanship, has been passed down through the ages to this day.Its taste can be felt with a fine texture that is sharp of after. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

As we refine our brewing techniques and pursue the path of a true brewer, we aim not only to grow as individuals but also to cultivate character. breweries.name: Imanishi Shuzou
breweries.address: 510, Miwa, Sakurai Shi, Nara Ken, 633-0001, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1660. For over 360 years, we have been crafting sake under the names Mimurosugi in Miwa, Nara—a land revered as the home of the god of sake.Nestled at the foot of Japan’s oldest shrine, Ōmiwa Shrine, a place worshiped since ancient times as the abode of the "god of sake" and the "deity of brewing," we dedicate ourselves to the art of sake brewing.Our brewing water comes from a well within the brewery, sourced from the sacred spring water of Mount Miwa, the shrine’s deity. The rice we use is cultivated by contract farmers along the same water vein as our brewing water, ensuring a harmonious connection between the land and the sake.We are honored and proud to brew sake in one of the world’s most extraordinary spiritual homes for sake. With this in mind, we remain committed to crafting sake that embodies the essence of Miwa. Brewery tours and tastings are not availableBusiness Hours:9:00 AM – 5:00 PMBusiness Days:Open every day except SundaySando Store:Tastings are available on weekdays (excluding busy periods)

breweries.description: In 1878, we started brewing sake near the first torii gate of Tanzan Shrine in Sakurai, which is famous for its autumn leaves.The brewery is located in front of Shorinji Temple, which is home to the national treasure, the eleven-faced Kannon, and has been blessed with beautiful water since ancient times.The eleven-faced Kannon (Goddess of Mercy) came from Miwa, so it is said that the God of Water and the God of Sake also reside in the Kannon holding a water bottle.Using local rice and local water, our motto is to brew sake that will please everyone. breweries.name: Kikutsukasa Jouzou
breweries.address: 555, Ozecho, Ikoma Shi, Nara Ken, 630-0223, Japan
breweries.description: In 1705 (Hoei 2), in Oze—a former post town on the Nara Kaido connecting Osaka and Nara—our brewery was founded by the first-generation Tohbei. In 2005, we celebrated our 300th anniversary.Today, the 13th-generation owner personally serves as the brewmaster, dedicating himself to crafting sake with meticulous care. With a focus on high-polish rice, handmade production, small-batch brewing, and bottle pasteurization, we continue to prioritize quality and tradition in every step of the process. breweries.name: Kitaoka Honten
breweries.address: 61, Kamiichi, Yoshinocho, Yoshino Gun, Nara Ken, 639-3111, Japan
breweries.description: "Kitoka Honten" is a long-established sake brewery founded in the first year of the Meiji era, located at the entrance to the Yoshino-Kumano National Park in southern Nara Prefecture. Its signature sake, "Yatagarasu," is named after the mythical sacred bird from Japanese legends. The water used to brew "Yatagarasu" is "Oku-Yoshino Forest Water," drawn from small streams flowing through the deep mountains of Yoshino. This water, low in iron and well-balanced in minerals, contributes to the creation of sake with gentle aromas and flavors. Leveraging these water characteristics, the brewery emphasizes harmony between flavor and aroma, crafting sake under the concept of "the more you drink, the better it tastes." By bringing out the full umami of the rice with a modest fragrance, their sake pairs perfectly as a table drink.Currently, the brewery is introducing a new line, the "Yatagarasu Core Series," exclusively available at select retailers, featuring a fresh design of the Yatagarasu by the brewmaster.The brewmaster's Instagram: @yatagarasu_sake_official Direct Sales StoreAccess:・15-minute walk from Kintetsu "Yamato Kamiichi Station."・By car: Located along Route 169 (near Yoshino Town Hall, across Yoshino River).Information:・Sake tastings are available. Brewery tours require a reservation.・Open on weekdays and Saturdays from 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM.・Online shopping available.Website:https://www.kitaoka-honten.com

breweries.name: Hikami Seishu
breweries.address: 1222, Yokota, Okuizumocho, Nita Gun, Shimane Ken, 699-1832, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1712 and with a history of more than 300 years, it is a sake brewery located in Oku-Izumo, Shimane Prefecture. We use the slightly soft water of the Hii River, which originates from Mt. Sentsu, a sacred peak, and local sake rice grown by one of the leading rice regions in western Japan, and brew sake while being supported by the harsh but rich nature of Oku-Izumo.The sake brewing that we cherish is the moderate aroma, mellow umami, and good aftertaste. It is characterized by a well-balanced sake that goes well with any dish and does not get tired of drinking.This brewery has also contributed to the development of Japan sake manufacturing technology and improved productivity, and is also known as the brewery where "foamless yeast", which is the mainstream in modern sake brewing, was discovered. rice grown in one of the most famous rice-producing areas in western Japan. Business hours are weekdays 9:00-17:00 (closed Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays). For specific business days, please refer to the business day calendar listed on the website.Sake is sold at the brewery.If you wish to tour the brewery and sample sake, please consult with us in advance. breweries.name: Tanabetakeshita Shuzou
breweries.address: 955-5, Kakeya, Kakeyacho, Unnan Shi, Shimane Ken, 690-2701, Japan
breweries.description: Tabe Takeshita Brewery took over the business from Takeshita Honten due to the lack of a successor and newly opened in June 2022. Production began in November with up-and-coming chief sake maker brewer Mr. Hamasaki.The history of the Takeshita family and the Tabe family, which took over the business, goes back to 1866, when the Takeshita family took over the brewing rights "Sakeza" from the Tabe family, one of the largest landowners in Japan, and started sake brewing.Later, the Takeshita family became a prominent family representing the region, producing the 74th Prime Minister Noboru Takeshita, and grew into a company that led the region's development. However, the business faced a crisis due to the lack of a successor, and the Takeshita family approached the Tabe family for advice, which led to the succession of the business.The concept is "fruity and outstandingly fresh sake", and the image is that of a sparkling clear sake from the summer sea in the San-in region.The first release in January 2023, Okuizumo Maemawamaya "test brew" was truly a test brew, as it was the first time for the new chief sake maker to work with the land, water, and climate. Based on last year's experience, the process was further brushed up and re-released as the "Rihachi" brand. With the up-and-coming chief sake maker Hamasaki, the Tabe family now once again took on the challenge of brewing sake. Sake brewery tours are available at Kakeya Sake Brewery Museum from 10:00-16:00.Closed (Mondays and New Year's holidays).Reservations required for tasting.
breweries.visiting_info: Sake brewery tours are available at Kakeya Sake Brewery Museum from 10:00-16:00.Closed (Mondays and New Year's holidays).Reservations required for tasting.

breweries.description: Fuji Shuzo brews sake "Izumo Fuji" in Izumo, the birthplace of Japan sake, with traditional handmade with take care.The name Izumo Fuji was given to the desire to make the best sake in Japan that is loved like Mt. Fuji in the land of Izumo.The fermentation mash that has been fermented slowly and carefully at a low temperature is gently and slowly squeezed in an old-fashioned wooden tank.Izumo Fuji, which is brewed with no effort and time in this way, is finished in a sake with a unique pure and honest taste that lies beyond harmony, and connects people and food with a good relationship. We are open weekdays from 8:30~17:30.We sell our products in our store.

breweries.name: Wakabayashi Shuzou
breweries.address: Ro-73, Kohama, Yunotsucho, Ooda Shi, Shimane Ken, 699-2511, Japan
breweries.description: Wakabayashi Sake Brewery is a small sake brewery located in Onsenzu-cho, Ota City, Shimane Prefecture, founded in 1869. Onsenzu-cho has long been known as a hot spring town, famous for the quality of its hot springs.It also prospered as a port for transporting silver from the Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine during the medieval and modern periods.Because the brewery is located near the sea, the brewing water used for sake is not from a well, but from a mountain 4 km away from the brewery.The rice used to make sake is mainly Yamada-Nishiki, which is purchased from contract farmers in the prefecture, and many other rice varieties suitable for sake brewing produced in Shimane Prefecture are also used. The sake is named after Li Bai, a Tang Dynasty poet who called himself "Sake Sen," and was named by Mr. Reijiro Wakatsuki, former Prime Minister of Japan and a native of Matsue City.We hope you will enjoy it with your daily meals and as a communication tool. breweries.name: Ochi Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 664-4, Shimoazae, Maniwa Shi, Okayama Ken, 716-1433, Japan
breweries.description: In 1893 (Meiji 26), in Shimoazae, Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture (formerly Hokubo Town in Jobo District), Shintaro Ochi, the first-generation founder, established a sake brewing business in pursuit of high-quality water. This area of Hokubo has long flourished as a hub connecting the regions of Bichu and Mimasaka (and was also an enclave of Kameyama Domain in Ise, Mie Prefecture).The surroundings of the brewery are steeped in history and natural beauty. Nearby are significant archaeological sites, such as the Arakiyama Burial Mound from the 3rd to 4th centuries and the Ohya No. 1 Burial Mound and Sadakita Burial Mound from the late 7th century, both designated as National Historic Sites. The area is also home to numerous natural wonders, including the Kanachiana and Suwa no Ana caves in Maniwa City, as well as Makido and Ikurado caves in Niimi City, which are designated as Okayama Prefecture Natural Monuments.Behind the brewery flows the Bichu River, a tributary of the Asahikawa River, where visitors can witness a spectacular display of fireflies (Genji, Heike, and Hime fireflies) over a stretch of 5 kilometers from late May to June. Surrounded by such rich nature, the brewery is nestled in a truly remarkable environment.[Brewery's Philosophy]At the heart of our brewing philosophy is the use of rice grown exclusively in Okayama Prefecture and the challenge of creating sake that fully utilizes the unique characteristics of natural medium-hard water. This water, sourced from the karst terrain feeding Suwa no Ana and Makido caves, is rich in calcium, magnesium, and phosphorus.We embrace the philosophy of "never cutting corners," inheriting the meticulous craftsmanship passed down from previous generations. By deeply reflecting the soil and natural environment of our brewery’s locale, we strive to create sake that can only be made here. Our guiding principle is to continually refine our individuality and dedicate ourselves to enhancing the quality of our sake. Business Hours: 9:00 AM to 6:30 PMClosed on: Tuesdays (additional temporary closures may apply).Brewery Tours: Not available.

breweries.description: Watanabe Shuzo was founded in 1909 (Meiji 42) in Tsurajima, Kurashiki City, a region reclaimed through land reclamation projects.More than 100 years later, the brewery retains the charm of a traditional sake brewery, with its namako-kabe (traditional plastered walls), a brick chimney, and a sugidama (cedar ball) hanging at the storefront. These features preserve the timeless beauty of Japan’s sake-making heritage. breweries.description: Founded in 1913 by the first-generation Chojuro Fujii in Yorishima Town, Okayama Prefecture, Kamikokoro Shuzou has remained dedicated to pursuing the delicious umami flavor of rice.Now under the leadership of the fifth-generation Nobuhiko Fujii, the brewery continues to honor the traditional taste of Kamikokoro while embracing new techniques to explore the untapped potential of rice as it embarks on creating the next 100 years of its history.As sake makers, we are manufacturers, but in everyday life, we are consumers. That’s why we would never create anything we wouldn’t serve to our own family. breweries.address: 163-1, Nariwacho Shimohina, Takahashi Shi, Okayama Ken, 716-0101, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1886, the brewery in Okayama believes that true local sake embodies a harmonious trinity of local rice, water, and craftsmanship. All the rice used is sourced exclusively from Okayama Prefecture, and the brewing water comes from the underground streams nourished by the Takahashi River system, one of the region's three major rivers. Under the expert techniques of the traditional Bichu Toji brewers, the sake reflects the bountiful blessings, rich traditions, and natural beauty of Okayama. The brewery continues to craft sake that embodies the essence of this fertile land. breweries.description: Kikuchi Shuzo, the sake brewery of "Sanzen" and "Kiseki-no- Sake", was founded in 1878 and is located in the port town of Bicchu Tamashima, which once prospered with the passing of the Sengoku boats.Using rice suitable for sake brewing, such as "Yamada-Nishiki", the highest grade of sake rice, and "Omachi", the pride of Okayama, and water from the Takahashi River system, which boasts excellent water quality, the company continues to take on the challenge of quality-oriented sake brewing with the delicate and outstanding skills of its chief sake brewery makers.As a result, the company has received numerous awards both in Japan and abroad, and is actively investing in facilities to improve quality and exporting sake overseas. breweries.address: 3734, Akitsucho Mitsu, Higashihiroshima Shi, Hiroshima Ken, 739-2402, Japan
breweries.description: Akitsu Town, where our brewery is located, is a small port town facing the serene and beautiful Seto Inland Sea. It is also known as the birthplace of ginjo sake, thanks to the development of the "soft water brewing method" by Miura Senzaburo, who named our sake brand.At Fukucho, we hold Miura’s words, “Hundreds of trials, thousands of revisions,” close to our hearts. While respecting the long-standing traditions of ginjo sake, we are also embracing innovative approaches to expand the world of sake. These include reviving the extinct native rice variety Hattanso and utilizing white koji and Satake’s “Shingin” rice polishing technology. The brewing water, sourced from the underground springs of the Saba River, is exceptionally soft, and since our founding, we have consistently crafted sake known for its smooth texture and rich flavor.Our motto, "Brewing the Season with Harmony," reflects our commitment to three forms of harmony: harmony among people, balance in flavor, and the connection with those who enjoy our sake. With this in mind, we deliver fresh, newly pressed Wakamusume sake every month. breweries.address: 93, Myoken, Mimacho, Mima Shi, Tokushima Ken, 771-2106, Japan
breweries.description: Our brewery is located in Mima Town, Tokushima Prefecture, a region blessed with abundant natural beauty and pristine water, including the renowned Mt. Tsurugi, Shikoku’s largest river Yoshino, and one of Japan’s clearest streams, the Anabuki River. Established in 1896 in the historically rich area of Kirai, which dates back to the Kamakura period, our brewery carries on the craftsmanship of the traditional Awa Toji brewers.Led by the skillful Awa Toji, who inherit these time-honored techniques, we use only locally sourced rice and are committed to brewing junmai sake with meticulous, traditional methods. As a brewery that exclusively produces sake from rice and rice malt, we continue to strive for excellence. From the land of joy, we deliver a cup of joy. breweries.address: 3279-1, Uchiko, Uchikocho, Kita Gun, Ehime Ken, 791-3301, Japan
breweries.description: Located in Uchiko Town, Ehime Prefecture, nestled in a mountainous rural area. Uchiko, a town of 16,000 residents, is known for its preserved streetscapes from the Edo to Meiji periods, making it a popular tourist destination. At the heart of this small town lies a quaint sake brewery founded in 1920 (Taisho 9) by a group of eight local sake brewers.The brewery utilizes sake rice grown by contracted local farmers and the abundant water sourced from the Odami Valley, which flows into the Oda River. Committed to traditional methods, all sake is handcrafted in the original wooden brewery building that has stood since its founding.Its flagship brand, Kyohina, is characterized by a subtle and elegant aroma. On the palate, it offers a harmonious balance of the rice's umami and a clean, crisp finish, making it an ideal companion for meals. Business Hours: 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM (except 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM)Closed on: Weekends, Obon holidays, and New Year holidays.Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.description: Founded in 1897, this traditional brewery continues to be committed to sake brewing that reflects the local environment. The spirit of this craftsmanship has been passed down through generations, and the result of their meticulous effort and time is a product that has repeatedly been honored with the highest gold award at the Annual Japan Sake Awards since it first received this accolade in 1989. Recently, the brewery has also focused on liqueurs made with plenty of fruits from Ehime Prefecture, known as the "Fruit Kingdom" (including plum, orange, yuzu, kiwi, etc.). The clean and refined taste crafted by the artisans and the toji has gained considerable popularity.
