breweries.address: 1299 Sakawacho, Takaoka Gun, Kochi Ken, 789-1201, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1603 (Keicho 8) in Sakawa, our brewery began as the personal sake producer for Shigeyoshi Fukao, who accompanied Kazutoyo Yamanouchi when he moved to Tosa.The name Tsukasabotan was later bestowed by Count Mitsuaki Tanaka, a notable samurai from Sakawa and the leader of the Rikuentai militia following the deaths of Ryoma Sakamoto and Shintaro Nakaoka. Tanaka declared, "Botan (peony) is the king of all flowers, and this sake shall be the leader among them."Known as the brewery most closely associated with Ryoma Sakamoto, we have been crafting sake for over 400 years, loved by Tosa's great historical figures. Tsukasabotan is a classic dry sake that embodies the spirit of Tosa, enhancing and elevating the flavors of fine cuisine. ・Tsukasa Botan Sake Gallery HoteiBusiness Hours:9:30 AM – 1:00 PM, 1:45 PM – 4:30 PM (lunch break from 1:00 PM to 1:45 PM)Closed on: Only during New Year holidays.Brewery Tours and Tasting:Available on weekdays only; reservation required.

breweries.name: Arimitsu Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 38-1, Akano, Aki Shi, Kochi Ken, 784-0033, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1903, our brewery's main brewing facility is even older, housed in a building constructed during the Edo period, which has been carefully repaired and improved over time.The name Akitora is inspired by Aki Kunitora, a samurai who ruled the eastern region of present-day Kochi Prefecture during the Sengoku period (16th century).Surrounded by the sea, mountains, and rivers, we craft sake in harmony with nature, aiming to produce gentle, food-friendly sake that serves as "nourishment for the soul." Business Hours:Weekdays 8:00 AM – 5:00 PMBrewery Tours and Tasting:Not currently available.(Retail sales only)

breweries.name: Tosa Shuzou
breweries.address: 418, Tai, Tosacho, Tosa Gun, Kochi Ken, 781-3521, Japan
breweries.description: Since founded in 1877 in the Tosa-Minehoku region, a mountainous area in northern Kochi Prefecture, we have been striving to produce high quality sake amidst abundant water and clean air. Tosa-cho, Tosa-gun, Kochi Prefecture, where the brewery is located, is surrounded by the source of the Yoshino River, which flows through the center of Shikoku, and by the serene natural environment of Lake Hayamiura, known as the water bottle of Shikoku.We are committed to brewing sake with rice grown in the local terraced rice paddies, and to using traditional techniques such as the "Namo Hashi" and "Yamahai" methods to create Kaygetsu as a "Sake of the land". Open 9:00～17:00 at Monday to Sunday(Except 1st January)We recommend to make a Reservation with email or phone.

breweries.name: Wakatakeya Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 706, Tanushimarumachi Tanushimaru, Kurume Shi, Fukuoka Ken, 839-1233, Japan
breweries.description: Since the founding of Wakatakeya by its first-generation, Denbei, over 320 years ago, we have carried forward his passion for sake brewing, honing our craft and building upon this rich history. Born in the land known as "Tanoshimaru," meaning "born in joy," our sake has been cherished for easing sorrow and anger while doubling happiness. It is a sake that nurtures the energy needed for tomorrow, a local brew that has lived alongside the joys and sorrows of the people of this region. Please savor this sake, deeply rooted in our community and its emotions. On-site Store "Genroku Gura"Business Hours:9:00 AM – 5:00 PMEnjoy tasting and shopping inside the historic brewery, built in 1700, the year after our founding.

breweries.description: Founded in 1893 as a distillery specializing in authentic shochu, we have focused primarily on barley-based shochu due to the Chikugo Plain's status as a major barley production area.We are committed to exploring the full potential of barley, exemplified by our Roasted Barley Shochu, crafted with roasted barley to create a distinctive flavor profile.Additionally, with a desire to share the art of authentic shochu with the world as part of the Japan Craft Spirits movement, we continuously strive to expand our expertise across various categories of distilled spirits. Please contact us by e-mail or through our website at least two weeks in advance to request a tour.Please note that we may not be able to accommodate your request due to the time of year or other factors.

breweries.name: Kikubijin Shuzou
breweries.address: 183, Setakamachi Kaminosho, Miyama Shi, Fukuoka Ken, 835-0025, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1735 (Edo period). The current brewery owner is the ninth generation. The brewery's name at the time was "Satsuma-ya." Kayo, the wife of the sixth-generation brewery owner, Kisaburo Ezaki, was the older sister of Hakushu Kitahara, the poetic sage of Yanagawa, and the eldest daughter of Nagataro Kitahara of Kitahara Shuzojo. In 1941, Hakushu, accompanied by his wife, children, and disciples, traveled westward to his hometown of Yanagawa and to the summit of Mt. Kiyomizu in Setaka Town, where he hosted a grand poetry gathering. He drank the sake, "Kikubijin," and wrote the name in bold strokes on three hanging scrolls, which became widely known. Since then, "Kikubijin" has been associated with Hakushu's naming, and the public continues to recognize it as such.The brewing water comes from the subterranean water of the Yabe River, which flows beside the brewery. The rice used is carefully selected sake rice, including Yamada Nishiki and Yume Ikkon from the Chikugo region, brewed with the dedicated skill of the Yanagawa toji.The brewing water comes from the subterranean water of the Yabe River, which flows beside the brewery. The rice used is carefully selected sake rice, including Yamada Nishiki and Yume Ikken from the Chikugo region, brewed with the dedicated skill of the Yanagawa toji (master brewer).From 2019 to 2023, the brewery won the Gold Award at the Fukuoka National Tax Agency Sake Appraisal. In 2021 and 2022, they received the Fukuoka Prefectural Governor's Award and the Fukuoka Prefectural Assembly Chairman's Award at the Fukuoka Sake Appraisal. In 2023, they won the IWC Gold Award, among many others.Their sake is loved by sake enthusiasts in local. Business Hours:8:00 AM – 5:00 PMBusiness Days:Weekdays (excluding weekends and public holidays)Brewery Tours:Reservation requiredTours include an explanation of the brewery's history, tasting, and sales.Access inside the brewery facility is not available.

breweries.description: Founded in 1898, our brewery is located in the heart of the Chikugo Plain, an area along the Chikugo River known for its pure water and fertile land. This region has long been a hub for sake production.Our mission is to provide happiness beyond the taste of "deliciousness" through the creation of uniquely flavorful sake, contributing to the enrichment of dining experiences.Our brewery specializes exclusively in junmai sake, using only locally sourced ingredients from Fukuoka Prefecture. We are also committed to a sustainable cycle of "rice farming → sake brewing → shochu production from sake lees → rice farming → …," reflecting our dedication to both quality and environmental responsibility. On-site Store: "Mori no Hanare"Business Hours:12:00 PM – 5:00 PMClosed on Sundays and public holidaysProduct Sales:Sake-related exhibits available. breweries.description: Beniotome Shuzo manufactures barley shochu, rice shochu, spirits, liqueurs, etc., with the one and only distilled spirit, Sesame Shochu Beniotome as its main brand. Haruno Hayashida, the founder of Beniotome Shuzo, married into a Japan sake brewery in Tajimarumachi, Fukuoka Prefecture, and aimed to brew a completely new type of sake. In 1978, we arrived at shochu with a fragrant aroma made with sesame seeds.Please enjoy the subterranean water of the blessings of the Mt. Mino range and the sesame shochu created by a female brewer. Tastings are available at the Benitome-no Jyoryu-sho direct sales store (open 9:00-17:00, closed during the year-end and New Year holidays).The facility is open for tours, but please contact us to make a reservation for group tours.

breweries.description: In recent years, the sake brewery has been increasingly nicknamed "Hakata Hyakunen Kura".As the only remaining "active sake brewery" in Hakata, it is a place where sake is still brewed in the olden days, and at the same time, as a historic space that remains in the city, it is also a place where receptions, concerts, and other events are held.The traditional white-walled storehouse with brick chimneys is a familiar part of the cityscape, and is designated as a Tangible Cultural Property of Japan. Sake brewery tours are not available.The production and sales office is open 11:00-17:00 (closed Tuesdays).Tastings are available.We do not offer tours of the brewery.For groups of 10 or more, please make a reservation in advance. breweries.description: Located alongside Kyushu's largest river, the Chikugo River, our brewery takes its name from the river's youthful, dynamic waves, symbolizing a commitment to continually creating new ripples.The concept behind our sake is "the push and pull of waves"—a bold surge of flavor that gently recedes, leaving a refreshing finish.Our brewery is home to two certified sake evaluation specialists, a distinction held by only two individuals in the prefecture. We are dedicated to crafting sake that passes rigorous quality inspections, ensuring that every bottle delivers absolute trust and satisfaction. With unwavering focus, we pour our hearts into creating exceptional sake. ・ShopBusiness Hours:9:00 AM – 5:00 PMClosed on Saturdays, Sundays, and public holidaysTasting:Not available・Brewery ToursReservation required (available outside of the brewing season only from July to September)

breweries.description: Our brewery was founded in 1875 by Genzo, known as a master swordsman, on the banks of the Chikugo River, the largest river in Kyushu. Genzo developed innovative brewing methods and made significant contributions to sake production in Fukuoka Prefecture.Building on this tradition, we continue to honor our heritage while constantly challenging ourselves to create new and exciting sake. Our goal is to craft products that deliver fresh surprises and emotional resonance for our customers.We have developed unique and distinctive products using proprietary techniques, such as sake brewed with black koji and Japan’s first jelly-like plum wine. breweries.address: 2-11-1, Umi, Umi Machi, Kasuya Gun, Fukuoka Ken, 811-2101, Japan
breweries.description: Our brewery is located on the outskirts of Fukuoka City, surrounded by mountains and rivers in a beautiful natural setting. Since its founding in 1792 by the first-generation Sakugoro Kobayashi, we have consistently upheld the tradition of crafting "delicious sake" through eight generations.During the time of the second-generation Shohei, the family came across an unusually shaped stone resembling an old turtle. Inspired by the proverb "A turtle lives for ten thousand years," they named their house sake Bandai ("Ten Thousand Generations").Although we are a small-scale brewery, we take pride in our carefully preserved techniques, using meticulous, handcrafted methods to produce our sake. On-site Store: Bandai Sake KoboBusiness Hours:10:00 AM – 6:00 PMClosed on WednesdaysBrewery Tours: Available on weekdays for groups of 5 or more (reservation required)

breweries.description: Located in Toho Village, on the eastern edge of Fukuoka Prefecture, Kataoka Shuzou has been crafting handmade sake with care since 1898 (Meiji 31), using the high-quality water and rice from the Hikosan mountain range. Among its offerings, the Junmai Ginjo Sake Toho Ikkon, brewed with Iwaya Spring Water—recognized as one of the Heisei Era's Top 100 Famous Waters—and terraced rice grown with this water, stands out as a rich and deeply flavorful masterpiece.With the desire to revitalize the local community, we enjoy working alongside a group of local residents who join us in the rice-growing and sake-making process.Every year on the last Sunday of April, we hold a Kurabiraki (brewery open house) event, where visitors can enjoy sake using unique ceramic sake vessels, a specialty of this region known for its pottery. breweries.description: Tanoshimaru Brewery is located in Tanoshimaru-cho, Kurume City, in the south-central part of Fukuoka Prefecture, a land full of nature with the Chikugo River to the north and the Mt. Mino Range to the south.The ingredients are carefully selected tow-row barley (some of which is grown under cooperative agreement in Hokkaido) polished to 65%, and the water is drawn from 70 meters underground from the subsoil of the Mt. Mino Range, selected as one of the 100 best waters in Japan.All of the shochu is prepared by hand and aged.Type of shochu produced: Honkaku shochu "Waramugi" and "Kurowaramugi". breweries.name: Kenjou
breweries.address: 1089, Sakaeda, Tachiarai Machi, Mii Gun, Fukuoka Ken, 830-1214, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Located in the central and southern part of Fukuoka Prefecture, about an hour's drive south of Fukuoka City, is the town of Tachiarai, Mitsui County.The Chikugo River flows majestically through the fertile and vast Chikugo Plain, with the Mt. Mino range in the background, we developed a carrot shochu called "Mezurashi" using ginseng cultivated in the rice carrot and fields along the Chikugo River and its flowing water.(Patented)The Chikugo Plain is a region of flourishing double cropping, where wheat is grown along with rice, and is one of the largest wheat-growing regions in Japan, second only to Hokkaido.We have also developed our own roasted barley shochu using barley from the Chikugo Plain, and currently sell representative brands such as "Fukurou" and "Kofukurou".We manufacture shochu with unparalleled characteristics, and we aim to produce unique and distinctive shochu, carefully making each bottle one by one, so that our shochu can be enjoyed and drunk for many years to come. Weekdays from 9:30 to 11:30 or 13:30 to 16:00Please contact us by phone, fax, or email about a month before the desired date of the visit to make an appointment.

breweries.name: Shichiyou Shuzou
breweries.address: 715, Ooaza Utsura, Okagakimachi, Enga Gun, Fukuoka Ken, 811-4203, Japan
breweries.description: In the first year of Ansei (1854), Keijiro Hoshikuma, who was engaged in the wax essential oil making business, started a sake brewing business in Setaka Shimonosho. Since then, we have continued to brew in the land of Setaka, but with the aim of further improvement, we have moved the brewery to the land of Okagaki Uchiura in search of pure brewing water.Taking this opportunity, the company name was changed to Shichiyo Shuzo Co., Ltd., and while inheriting the tradition that has continued for about 160 years since the founding of Hoshikuma Shuzo, a new challenge began at the foot of Mt. Kiwatama in the 4th year of Reiwa. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

Another notable offering is Saro, a refined gyokuro tea-infused shochu made with premium tea leaves sourced from Kyoto, delivering a sophisticated flavor experience. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.name: Gochouda Shuzou
breweries.address: 2081, Shiotacho Gochoda, Ureshino Shi, Saga Ken, 849-1425, Japan
breweries.description: We brew sake in the Gomachida district of Ureshino City, which is located in the southwestern part of Saga Prefecture. Founded in 1922, we are making Japan sake for more than 100 years. The representative brand is "Azumaichi", with the hope of aiming to be the best sake in the East. Since 1988, we have been cultivating sake rice "Yamada Nishiki" in-house in the vicinity of the brewery, and we carefully grow it at a cultivation association centered on brewers and farmers, and we are making sake with a focus on Yamada Nishiki.The philosophy is "people, rice, and work together to brew good sake", and the sake quality we aim for is "food that harmonizes the balance of the five flavors and sake that is close to the heart". Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.address: 2421, Hamamachi, Kashima Shi, Saga Ken, 849-1322, Japan
breweries.description: At Mitsutake Shuzoujou, we uphold the philosophy that “crafting sake means cultivating people.” Guided by the motto “innovation within tradition,” we strive to adapt to the times while preserving the time-honored traditions of sake-making. We are committed to continuously improving the quality of each product through unwavering effort.For many years, we have focused on the production of sake and shochu. Moving forward, we aim to expand into the manufacturing of fermented foods alongside our core products.We place great importance on personal growth and aspire to become a brewery that inspires and moves people. By creating meaningful contributions to food culture and conducting honest, heartfelt business, we seek to deliver value and sincerity in everything we do. breweries.description: A sake brewery founded in the Bunka era (1804-1818).The main building is a machiya-style house, estimated to have been built in the late Edo period.The brick chimney is a symbol of the sake brewery, and other buildings used in the brewing process from the Meiji to Showa periods are still in existence. Available on business days (Monday through Saturday) from 10:00 to 16:00. For groups, please make a reservation in advance.The time required is about 30 minutes for a guided tour and tasting (free of charge).

breweries.name: Tenzan Sake Brewery
breweries.address: 1520, Iwakura, Ogimachi, Ogi Shi, Saga Ken, 845-0003, Japan
breweries.description: Since 1875, when Tsuki and Toshizo Shichida transitioned from flour milling and noodle-making to sake brewing, the brewery has continued its journey and will celebrate its 150th anniversary in 2025.Guided by the philosophy of "Fueki Ryuko" (continuity and change), the brewery cherishes the enduring values ("Fueki") of its tradition—maximizing the blessings of the natural environment of Ogi to produce sake of uncompromising quality. At the same time, embracing the principle of "Ryuko" (change), it remains committed to fearless and innovative challenges that align with the evolving times. Business Hours:10:00 AM – 4:00 PMOpen on weekdays, except during New Year holidays (irregular closures on Saturdays)Free space for viewing, Paid tastings available(We offer spaces such as those showcasing old wooden barrels once used.)You can experience a 3D virtual brewery tour from the following URL:https://tenzan.co.jp/virtual/

breweries.description: We brew sake, authentic shochu, and ume (plum) wine using the legendary water that was used for tea party by the ancient Japanese ruler "Taikoh Hideyoshi".The sake brewed with the soft brewing water is gentle on the palate and runs smoothly down the throat.The brewery's flagship sake, "Jyuraku Taikoh Daiginjo" has won the Gold Award at the National New Sake Competition a total of 12 times. breweries.address: 785, Yamagawa, Ariecho, Minamishimabara Shi, Nagasaki Ken, 859-2202, Japan
breweries.description: Yoshidaya was founded in 1917 (Taisho 6) and is located in Arie Town, Minami-Shimabara City, in the southern part of the Shimabara Peninsula, renowned for its pristine water sources.We use water from our private well, fed by the spring water of Unzen's underground streams, as our brewing water. Our sake is pressed using the rare hanegi-shibori technique, which has become increasingly uncommon. Additionally, we brew sake with flower yeast derived from blossoms, creating unique flavors. breweries.description: Founded in 1688 (Genroku 1), our brewery is located in the southern part of Hirado Island, a region renowned for its beautiful sea, picturesque scenery, and deep historical legacy.Our founder, Chojibemon Fukuda, passed down the philosophy, “Sake is crafted with the heart and nurtured by the wind,” a tradition that continues to this day.Looking ahead, we are embarking on the challenge of creating sake that embodies the essence of the Hirado sea. breweries.name: Genkai Shuzou
breweries.address: 550-1, Gonouracho Shiharanishifure, Iki Shi, Nagasaki Ken, 811-5125, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Iki Island is said to be the birthplace of barley shochu, and barley shochu has been produced there since the 16th century.The name of our brewery is Iki where is name of place and the name of our sake is "Mugi-Shochu Iki".It was in 1900 that we started shochu making using the underground water from the highest mountain in Iki (Gaku-no-Tsuji).Made from barley and rice malt, "Mugi Shochu Iki" is characterized by the sweetness of the rice malt and the aroma of the barley. Shochu tasting (free of charge) and sales are available at the adjoining Shochu Museum from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm Monday through Saturday and from 9:30 am to 4:00 pm on Sundays.We are closed on August 15 and during the year-end and New Year holidays.

breweries.name: Yamanomori Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 85, Gonouracho Shiharanishifure, Iki Shi, Nagasaki Ken, 811-5125, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Our brewery was founded in 1897, the oldest in Iki, and celebrated its 124th anniversary this year.We make unrefined sake from rice and barley grown in Iki, using the traditional turtle brewing method, distill the matured unrefined sake under normal pressure, and store the finished sake in turtles or tanks to mature.The resulting sake is then stored in turtles or tanks to mature.The same turtling and long-term storage process that has been used since the company's founding give Yamanomori its deep flavor. Shochu tasting (free of charge) and sales are available at the adjoining Shochu Museum from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm Monday through Saturday and from 9:30 am to 4:00 pm on Sundays.We are closed on August 15 and during the year-end and New Year holidays.

breweries.name: Ooshima Shuzou
breweries.address: 830, Oshimacho, Saikai Shi, Nagasaki Ken, 857-2402, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: In the northern part of Nagasaki Prefecture, 18 km off the coast of Sasebo, Saikai Oshima is an island rich in nature surrounded by the beautiful blue sea and sky. We are particular about local Nagasaki raw materials, pursue higher quality and deeper taste, and make shochu with great care.All barley shochu is made from carefully selected two-row barley from Nagasaki Prefecture. At Imo Shochu, we own our own farm, grow and harvest potatoes, and continue to explore product development with the idea of "making shochu that can only be made here"."I want the people who drank it to be happy" we will deliver from Nagasaki and Saikai Oshima with that single-mindedness. Tasting and sales are available on weekdays from 10:00 to 12:00 and 13:00 to 16:00.Factory tours are available by appointment only.Please contact us by phone or e-mail.

breweries.address: 1432-1, Ashibecho Fukaehonmurafure, Iki Shi, Nagasaki Ken, 811-5326, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: On the shores of the bridge connecting Kyushu and the Chinese mainland lies the island of Iki, the birthplace of barley shochu. In a quiet corner of this island, the history of Iki Shochu continues with clarity and purity, embodied in a single drop of barley shochu: Saruko.Back in 1903 (Meiji 36), our founder established the distillery along the banks of the Saruko River, a picturesque sanctuary renowned for its pristine and abundant waters. These waters provide an ideal environment for making and aging shochu.The name Saruko originates from this very river, and our founder, inspired by its widespread recognition, adopted it as the name for this exceptional shochu. breweries.description: Founded in Meiji 28. Since that time, they have had their own rice fields and manufacture rice shochu with rice grown with their own hands. Since 1986, we have been making shochu with organically farmed rice. Following the organic certification system that started in 2001, all of our own fields and contract farmers have been certified. We value the soil, climate, and climate, and are engaged in sustainable organic farming and shochu production. breweries.description: Founded in 1900 as a Kuma shochu distillery with a proud 500-year tradition, we have been aiming at honkaku shochu making that is easy to drink and refreshing, while respecting the philosophy of "good rice, good water, and good people" in its production.Hakutake", "Hakutake Shiro", and "Hakutake KAORU", our representative brands of honkaku shochu, are easy-drinking shochu made using the vacuum distillation method, and are loved by fans around the world as a brand that has spread throughout Japan. breweries.name: Chiyonosono Shuzou
breweries.address: 1782, Yamaga, Yamaga Shi, Kumamoto Ken, 861-0501, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1896. The company was originally a rice wholesaler in Yamaga City, which prospered during the Edo period as a rice accumulation area and an inn town on the Buzen Highway.For the reason, the company's passion for rice and junmai sake was so strong that it pioneered the production and sale of junmai sake in Japan after World War II.In addition, we are continuing to take on the challenge of making sake, such as 1844ml bottled Nama Genshu and Daiginjo with a cork stopper.In this age of diversifying consumer tastes, we aim to create sake that is delicious to drink and that enriches the heart of the drinker. At the brewery, you can visit the historical museum displaying old brewing tools for free, and there is also a shop where you can purchase Chiyonosono sake, akazake, liqueurs, and mirin.Next to the shop, there is a standing bar where you can compare different Chiyonosono sake.Note: Tasting at the standing bar is paid.Note: Brewery factory tours are not available.Business Hours:[Shop & Historical Museum]Weekdays: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PMSaturdays, Sundays, and public holidays: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM[Standing Bar]Closed on Mondays and Wednesdays.Weekdays: 3:00 PM – 7:00 PMSaturdays, Sundays, and public holidays: 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM (until 7:00 PM if the next day is a holiday)Note: Business days and hours may change.For more details, please check Chiyonosono's official Instagram or contact us by email or phone.Chiyonosono Official Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/chiyonosono1896/

breweries.name: Matsu no Izumi Shuzou
breweries.address: 169-1, Uekita, Asagiricho, Kuma Gun, Kumamoto Ken, 868-0422, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Kuma and Hitoyoshi, located in Kumamoto, Kyushu. Here, in the Hori no Sumi area, pristine water emerges after passing through the granite of Mt. Shiraga in the Kyushu Mountains over many years.With over 500 years of Kuma Shochu history, Matsunoizumi Shuzo has faithfully carried on the shochu culture cultivated by our predecessors. Since our founding, we have preserved the traditional craftsmanship of Rice, Water, People, and Distillery.While cherishing this heritage, we remain dedicated to meticulously crafting even better shochu with care and precision. At the shochu distillery, you can taste aged and reserve shochu, and draw high-quality underground water used in the shochu-making process. This water is also used in the on-site dining area, where you can enjoy dishes like dago-jiru, made with fresh, locally sourced ingredients.

breweries.name: Kumamotoken Shuzoukenkyusyo
breweries.address: 1-7-20, Shimasaki, Chuo Ku, Kumamoto Shi, Kumamoto Ken, 860-0073, Japan
breweries.description: This laboratory was founded in 1909 to improve the quality of sake produced in Kumamoto Prefecture.It is a very rare brewery (research institute) in Japan that brews and sells sake "Kouro" and maintains, manages, and distributes Kumamoto yeast (original strain of Association No. 9 yeast).The brewery is said to have laid the foundation for today's ginjo production with its Noshira-style skylight (natural ventilation system), bag pressing, and double vat system (the prototype for thermal tanks). Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: The Kuma River is the largest river in Kumamoto Prefecture.Among the breweries that make Kuma shochu, we are located at the top of the Kuma River, blessed with fertile soil and clear underground water, and we continue to challenge ourselves by maintaining our traditions and adopting new techniques.There are about 1,300 storage barrels, and each barrel blends shochu to create its own unique flavor.Representative brands are "Onitaoshi" and "Oishi". Onitaoshi has been around since the company's founding and has long been loved by the locals, while Oishi is a gem that has been aged and stored for a long time. Weekdays 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. (free tasting)For reservations for tours, please contact us through our website.(Tours may not be available due to production conditions or other reasons).Tours time is about 30 minutes.

breweries.name: Mercian
breweries.address: 3-1, Sanrakucho, Yatsushiro Shi, Kumamoto Ken, 866-0017, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Located in Yatsushiro, Kumamoto, a region blessed with fertile land and pristine spring water, we have been dedicated to crafting unique flavors since its founding in 1939. From rice shochu to black sesame shochu, orange sweet potato shochu, and even craft gin made with Japanese citrus, we have pursued tastes found nowhere else.By harnessing the bounty of nature to its fullest, combining inherited techniques and passion with our one-of-a-kind 5 types of stills and 6 distillation pots, we are committed to creating new and unparalleled "deliciousness" that the world has never experienced before. breweries.name: Matsushita Jouzoujou
breweries.address: 2582, Iwano, Mizukami Mura, Kuma Gun, Kumamoto Ken, 868-0701, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Jihei, the Maker of Rice Shochu Since the Edo PeriodMatsushita Jouzoujou is a historic producer of Kumamoto's famous Kuma Shochu, founded during the Edo period. Shochu production began with the first-generation Jibei in Mizukami Village, part of the Sagara Domain, and has been passed down through 14 generations. Today, the 14th-generation head of the family, the first male heir born in 108 years, carries on the legacy.Locally Produced Rice Shochu, Starting with Rice CultivationThe brewery is committed to environmentally conscious rice farming, using Tachiharuka rice grown in collaboration with family farmers.The rice, cultivated in the rich natural environment of Mizukami Village, Kumamoto Prefecture, is grown with minimal use of chemical fertilizers and pesticides. This careful approach results in a deeply flavorful and environmentally friendly shochu. Brewery tours are available with advance notice.
breweries.visiting_info: Brewery tours are available with advance notice.

breweries.name: Fukuda Shuzou
breweries.address: 137-2, Nishiaida Shimomachi, Hitoyoshi Shi, Kumamoto Ken, 868-0072, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Founded in 1935 (Showa 10) in Hitoyoshi City, Kumamoto Prefecture, by Kunihiko Fukuda, originally from Reihoku Town, Amakusa District. The original brand name at the time of founding was Fuku no Tsuyu. In 1954 (Showa 29), Fukuda Sake Brewing was established, continuing its operations to the present day.In 1964 (Showa 39), the company introduced an automated koji-making system, achieving greater uniformity and enhanced saccharification power in the koji. To accommodate increased production, the brewery underwent a major renovation in 1982 (Showa 57). In 1984 (Showa 59), a vacuum distillation unit was introduced, enabling the production of shochu with a refined aroma and light, elegant flavor. breweries.description: Yotsuya Brewery was founded in 1919 by Kenpachi Yotsuya in Usa City, Oita Prefecture, overlooking the Suonada Sea, the home of Usa Hachiman in northern Kyushu. The passion for spirits (shochu) that has been passed down from generation to generation remains unchanged and continues to live on today. We continue to make shochu under the motto of "Creation and Challenge" with the techniques handed down since our founding, without ever betraying the support and expectations of our customers. breweries.name: Oochi Shuzou
breweries.address: 277-1, Kamiura Azamuiura, Saiki Shi, Oita Ken, 879-2601, Japan
breweries.description: Located in Saiki City, in the southernmost part of Oita Prefecture, Oochi Shuzou was established in 1885 (Meiji 18) as a sake brewery under the patronage of the Mouri family, who were the feudal lords of the time. The brewery operated in the castle town of Saiki Castle, which was ruled by the Mouri clan.After facing a succession crisis and the threat of closure, the business was revived in 2019 (Reiwa 1) when two brothers from the local area took over the management.With the theme of creating “subtle, aromatic, and richly flavored sake to complement meals,” Oochi Shuzou is committed to producing sake that enhances the dining experience.The story of the newly reborn Oochi Shuzou is far from over, with many dreams yet to be realized. breweries.description: Located near the border between Oita and Fukuoka prefectures and surrounded by the beautiful mountains of the Kyushu mountain range, Hita was once the domain of the Edo shogunate.Because of this, the burden of annual tribute was light, and the region is known as Kyushu's little Kyoto, where a unique and rich culture and industry grew up.Our company was founded in this Hita region in 1789, and the founder started sake brewing because the limpid water from the spring at the local Old Pine Shrine was suitable for sake brewing.This is the origin of the company name. breweries.description: Founded in 1907, our brewery originally produced sake. In 2003, the fourth-generation owner, Makoto Nakazono, launched the shochu brand JOTOKUYA. Through this approach, we continue to take on the challenge of crafting shochu that reflects the unique terroir of our home. From this hometown rich in nature, we have been studying every day with honest product creation in mind in order to transmit the handmade goodness and the commitment of the brewery, which cannot be imitated by national brands. breweries.name: Komatsu Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 3341, Nagasu, Usa Shi, Oita Ken, 872-0001, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1868, our brewery benefits from a unique environment that includes mineral-rich underground water with a hardness of 150, rice grown in the Usa Plain, and the winter monsoon winds. Although production was temporarily halted in 1988, it resumed in 2008 when the sixth-generation owner returned to the brewery. This marked the launch of a new junmai sake brand, Hojun.Since 2009, we have focused on reviving Oitamii, a heritage rice variety native to Oita Prefecture, and we are the only brewery in Japan to produce junmai sake exclusively using Oitamii. In 2018, we celebrated our 150th anniversary and the 10th anniversary of the Hojun brand. Business Hours:8:00 AM – 6:00 PMClosed on New Year’s Day, irregular holidaysBrewery tours by reservation; tastings available.

breweries.name: Himeizumi Shuzou
breweries.address: 3380-1, Iwaikawa, Hinokagecho, Nishiusuki Gun, Miyazaki Ken, 882-0402, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Located in Hinokage-cho, northern Miyazaki Prefecture, this shochu distillery was founded in 1831, and the current president is the seventh generation.It produces and sells honkaku shochu (sweet potato shochu), barley shochu, buckwheat shochu, and glutinous rice shochu.The brewery where we still brew shochu today is more than 130 years old, and the pillars and walls are pained black by the brewery yeast and koji mold.The water (for shochu making) is using the spring water from the opposite shore of the brewery.The brewery is still making sake by hand, from production to labeling. Please contact us by phone or e-mail at least one week in advance for a tour of the warehouse.(Available for tours Monday through Saturday, not on Sundays or holidays)Hours: 10:00-16:00 (approx. 40 min.)Free to attend, free tasting, direct sales available. Max. 30 people.

breweries.name: Sakagura Outemon
breweries.address: 898-8, Kitagocho Ofuji, Nichinan Shi, Miyazaki Ken, 889-2401, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: An esteemed Sakagura established in the 28th year of the Meiji era, surrounded by the Komatsuyama mountain range and the Inoyae Valley in Nichinan, Miyazaki Prefecture.We fully embrace the blessings of each season, dedicating ourselves to the art of shochu making with a spirit of daily exploration.We are committed to using only the finest ingredients, including “spring water, koji, yeast starter, mash, distillation, rice, sweet potatoes, and barley,” to carry on the time-honored craftsmanship of Shochu making, perfected by skilled artisans.We strive to be a Shochu cherished by those who appreciate our spirits, continuously devoting ourselves to improvement.Please savor the essence of the “craftsman’s soul” with us.Fifth Generation Master Sakagura, Shigeyoshi Takahashi 【Dates】Thursday, Friday, Saturday(Reservation required)※Please note that there are days when the brewery is closed due to our schedule.【Tour Duration】Approximately 40 minutes(Tour start time: 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM)※Please note that there are days when the brewery is closed due to our schedule.【Limit on the number of people】Limited to one group per day(maximum of 8 people)※For groups larger than 8 people, please contact us.【Shochu Sales】Shochu is available for purchase at the hall.※We also offer shipping to other regions.【Tour Course Fee】Price per person: 500 yen(tax included)※Basic tour course with special tasting included.Here is the flow of the brewery tour course. : https://ohtemon1895.jp/en/sakagura-tour/#kengaku【Blending Course】Price per person: 1,500 yen(tax included)※In addition to the brewery tour,you can experience blending at the hall.Here is the flow of the blending Course. : https://ohtemon1895.jp/en/sakagura-tour/#blendingTo provide easy-to-understand explanations of the shochu production process for beginners, we have prepared panels in multiple languages.※Supported languages (4 languages) : Japanese, English, Chinese (traditional and simplified)

breweries.description: It has been more than 130 years since founder Niemon Akashi started making shochu in 1891 in what is now Ebino City, Miyazaki Prefecture. In a natural environment suitable for making shochu surrounded by the Kyushu Mountains and the Kirishima Mountain Range, we are blessed with excellent raw materials and high-quality clear water, and we practice careful preparation using traditional brewing techniques to create "delicious shochu that will make you feel drunk to the core" is manufactured.We manufacture products such as our representative brand "Meigetsu'', "?Naina'', which is limited to our dealers, and "Tsuki no Megami'', a barley shochu stored in oak barrels. Weekday tours of the brewery are available from 9:00-12:00 and 13:00-17:00.(Approx. 40 min. Free to attend, free tasting).To request a tour, please contact us by phone or through our website.

breweries.name: Shouro Shuzou
breweries.address: 1-17-5, Terazato, Kushima Shi, Miyazaki Ken, 888-0011, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Founded in 1928, We are a small shochu brewery located in Kushima City in the southernmost part of Miyazaki Prefecture. Supported by local farmers, microorganisms that live in the brewery, and drinkers, we have been making shochu here for over 90 years. The foundation of the Shouro Shuzo concept is all about "something you can drink any time". Therefore, we dare not express any special strong aroma or other characteristics in the quality of our syochu liqueur, and we place great importance on creating a somewhat calming taste that gently accompanies the drinker. In recent years, while maintaining the tradition of the brewing method, we are challenging ourselves to use various raw materials such as sweet potato varieties and koji. Weekdays 8:00-17:00 Only products can be sold at the office.Brewery tours or tastings are not available.

breweries.description: Hokugo Town in Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture, where Sakura-no-sato Sake Brewery is located, is also known as "Cherry Town" because the Someiyoshino cherry trees bloom earlier than any other cherry tree in Japan, and is surrounded by rich nature with hot springs and clear streams.Although the brewery has a relatively short history since its establishment in 1994, it began long-term storage in large jars in 1997, and currently has over 5,000 jars in which to store and age its shochu. breweries.address: 45-1, Shoei cho, Miyazaki Shi, Miyazaki Ken, 880-0833 Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1967. The brewery that developed Japan's first soba shochu "Unkai". Centered on Ayakura, located in Ayamachi, Miyazaki Prefecture, which is registered as a UNESCO Eco Park, our skilled artisans craft authentic shochu with the flavor of the ingredients at Gokase Brewery, Takaoka Brewery, Kagoshima Izumi Brewery, and other authentic shochu breweries in various places in a rich natural environment with clear air and pure water. Representative brands are "Soba Shochu Unkai", "Kobiki BLUE" and "Iitomo", and Japan sake "Tokiichi", "Hatsumiyo" and "Kikuhatsumiyo". breweries.description: Since founded in 1916, we had continued to produce high-quality Honkaku Shochu made from obsessived sweet potatoes, water, rice, and other ingredients at the foot of the rich natural Kirishima mountain range. In addition to the production and sale of Honkaku Shochu such as "Kuro Kirishima (our representative brand which inherits the roots of the black koji brewing method used when the company was founded)", "Shiro Kirishima", and "Aka Kirishima", we also produce craft beer and operate a restaurant business at the "Kirishima Factory Garden". We will continue to focus on shochu production that is rooted in the local community and deliver delicious shochu. Kirishima Factory Garden offers tours of the shochu brewing factory. (5 times a day, approx. 60 minutes, free of charge, tasting available) Please reserve in advance via the Internet.

breweries.address: 5323, Kaguchiura, Takanabecho, Koyu Gun, Miyazaki Ken, 884-0004, Japan
breweries.description: Located in a rich natural setting with a view of the Hyuga Sea to the east and Mt. Ozuzu to the north, the Kurokabe Brewery is the main base for shochu production at Takara Shuzo, and was named "Kurokabe Brewery" because the walls of the brewery are jet black.In addition to producing authentic shochu such as "Ikkomon", "ISAINA" and "Yokaichi", full sweet potato shochu, the brewery also owns approximately 85 types and 20,000 barrels of "barrel-stored aged sake", which is blended into Kou-type shochu and chuhai, and is made using a collection of unique techniques developed over the years by Takara Shuzo, including a particular focus on distilling, aging, and blending The company's shochu production process is based on the unique technologies Takara Shuzo has developed over many years. brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Since its founding in 1909, Ochiai Shuzojo has been dedicated to producing authentic shochu. The distillery is located in a basin surrounded by mountains, with a view of Boroishiyama in the Wanizuka Mountain Range.Nearby, the pristine Kagamizu River flows, and its underground water serves as the source for the distillery’s brewing process. Since its inception, the brewery has been using traditional Kame—ceramic jars crafted approximately 200 years ago, each with a capacity of around 540 liters—for creating the fermentation starter.At Ochiai Shuzojo, our goal is to craft delicious shochu that customers can enjoy daily and form long-lasting relationships with. Business Hours:9:00 AM – 5:00 PMClosed:Saturdays, Sundays, and public holidaysBrewery Tours:Available by reservation

breweries.address: 21-25, Nazefurutacho, Amami Shi, Kagoshima Ken, 894-0016, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: The traditional three-stage semi-koji method is the basis of the brewing process, and all processes are done by hand.The distillation equipment used by Nishihira Honke is called a variable three-way valve distillation equipment and is very distinctive. This variable three-way valve can create a refined aroma and rich flavor by manipulating three lyne arms.Craftsmen spend a lot of time and effort to produce shochu by hand, which enhances the original flavor of brown suger shochu and gives it a sweet taste. Brewery tours and tastings are not available.

breweries.description: At Oosumi Shuzo, we preserve Kagoshima’s traditional methods of shochu production while incorporating distillation techniques developed by Suntory through their expertise in whisky-making. Through ongoing innovation, we strive to create new and delightful flavors.What we value most is craftsmanship that only humans can achieve. This commitment is evident in our meticulous process, from carefully selecting each sweet potato by hand to visually monitoring the progress of distillation with precision. This dedicated approach allows us to remove impurities and bitterness, resulting in a rich, sweet aroma and smooth flavor. Factory tours are unavailable.

breweries.name: Kojika Shuzou
breweries.address: 7312, Airacho Kamimyo, Kanoya Shi, Kagoshima Ken, 893-1101, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Founded in 1971 in Kanoya City, Kagoshima Prefecture, the company celebrated its 50th anniversary in August 2021.In order to secure sweet potatoes of stable quality, Kojika Brewery founded the Kojika Agricultural Production Association in 1994, ahead of other breweries in the prefecture. The establishment of the Production Association enabled thorough production control and stable supply of raw materials.The representative brand "Kojika" is characterized by its mild sweetness and clean taste, and is a shochu that can be enjoyed every evening without getting tired of it. Factory tours are available on our business days from 9:00 to 16:00 (takes about 1 hour).Free of charge. We need preparations, so please make a reservation in advance.

breweries.description: Founded in 1894 (Established in 1993). Representative brand name "Tekkan".Ogatama Shuzo is shochu making located near the "Tree of Ogatama", an over 800-year-old tree designated as a national natural monument in Satsumasendai City, where is carried out in the traditional way.Under the philosophy of "learning from the past", we are striving to revive the "good old" traditions as products that fit the current era, and to discover new things from the past history.We want to preserve not only our products, but also the local culture. We believe that our mission is to create a history of delicious shochu making that fits the times. After guiding you through the manufacturing process at the brewery and the barbarian sake brewery, you will have a tasting.Observation period: Production period from August to December.Visiting hours: 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. (about 1 hour)Please contact us by phone or email at least one week in advance to make a reservation.

breweries.description: Founded in 1910. It is a small brewery in size among the 112 breweries in Kagoshima.With 8 employees, we deliver Kagoshima's sweetpotato shochu to the whole country or the world.Most of the ingredients are produced in Osumi, Kagoshima. It is produced at an affiliated farm.In wine, it is a "chateau" or a "domaine (a producer who owns his own vineyard)".We brew in earthenware jars (pot, vessels), which have been handed down in Kagoshima since ancient times. We are a small but shochu brewery that preserves tradition and produces shochu and pursuing the real thing. Tours are available on weekdays from 10:00 to 16:00 (except 12:00 to 13:00). The name of the place and the name "Godai" were given by Godaiin, the Shinto shrine temple of Shinden Shrine, which is dedicated to Niniginomikoto, for its blessings, and "Satsuma Godai" inherits the venerable name and carries its history and traditional taste to the present day.The honkaku shochu "Kura no Kami" has a mild and elegant taste, and even with hot water or with water, it has a rich aroma of sweetpotato and a delicious taste that does not lose its firmness. For many years they have been making shochu, a traditional Japanese distilled spirit.The distillery features an all-potato shochu made with Kagoshima-grown Kogane-Sengan, which is also used for the malted rice.Kirishima, where the distillery is located, is a mountain range rich in nature and very famous place for its spring water.We hope you will enjoy the shochu produced by the craftsmen's skills and the nature of Kirishima. breweries.description: Founded in 1899 (Meiji 32), our small distillery is located in Haru, Akune City, Kagoshima Prefecture.For over 120 years, we have been crafting shochu, preserving its traditional, time-honored flavor while incorporating new techniques to carry our legacy into the future.One unique story that sets us apart is the revival of the Kabuto-kama Distillation Method, a technique used in Japan during the Edo to Meiji periods. Our fifth-generation owner, a former engineer, painstakingly restored this method through repeated trial and error, blending tradition with modern ingenuity.We meticulously select each sweet potato by hand and carefully cut them during preparation. Even the bottle labels are applied one by one with great care, ensuring that every step of the process reflects our dedication to quality. Business Hours:8:00 AM – 12:00 PM / 1:00 PM – 5:00 PMClosed:Sundays and public holidays(January to August: closed on Saturdays, Sundays, and public holidays)Brewery Tours:Available upon request; please contact us in advance.

breweries.description: The third generation head of the family, Masaaki Shinna, began producing and selling Okinawan-style awamori in 1920.In 1945 (Showa 20), the brewery was exposed to air raids and the entire brewery was destroyed by the war. The following year, with the help of rationing and support from relatives and friends, a temporary factory was built and distilled spirits were brewed using Awamori seed malt obtained from Okinawa.After that, efforts were made to improve the facilities, but in 1977, the damage caused by Typhoon Okinoerabu was so severe that the company relocated and built a new factory, where it continues to this day.The representative brand is "Tenkaichi". They are also engaged in shochu making with unique characteristics, such as "Imo Koji Imo", the industry's first 100% sweet potato shochu made with sweet potato koji, "Tsurunashigenji", a shochu made by reviving sweet potatoes that that was on the verge of extinction, "Yasuda" and "Flamingo Orange", both of which have a fruity aroma.A chief Sake Maker Nobuhisa Yasuda received an award from the Minister of Health, Labor, and Welfare as a "Contemporary Master Craftsman" in 2017. brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: "Satsuma Shuzo Kedogawa Distillery Meiji Brewery" is a brewery that has been in existence since the Meiji Era. It preserves the atmosphere of the late Meiji period, when the process of honkaku shochu production was established, and the breath of the craftsmen. Even today, a group of craftsmen, including a chief shochu maker, continue to make shochu in the traditional way, and visitors can observe the brewing process up close. The 99 earthenware jars (pot, vessels) in the museum are more than 100 years old and are still used for shochu making. Tours are available from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, and tastings are offered at the shop. Guided tours by staff are held three times daily at 11:00 AM, 1:30 PM, and 2:30 PM. Same-day participation is also available.

breweries.description: Founded in 1896, this shochu distillery has over 125 years of history and 13 employees.We make shochu from a wide variety of shochu making ingredients such as "Komatsu Tatewaki (sweet potato shochu)", "Kaikouzu", "Anno Imo" , as well as barley, rice, and corn.Our representative brand "Komatsu Tatewaki" is named after "Komatsu Tatewaki, a retainer of the Satsuma Clan", who is an ancestor of our company's owner.We will continue to devote ourselves to shochu making in order to live up to the name of "Komatsu Tatewaki", who contributed to the Meiji Restoration. We do not accept factory tours or tastings, but we do sell our products to the public at our factory (sales office).

breweries.description: Founded as a cooperative by five long-established shochu distilleries with origins in the Meiji era, Ibusuki Shuzo Cooperative was established to combine traditional techniques and begin shochu production on an unprecedented scale. The cooperative aimed to achieve ideal brewing methods to enhance quality and diversify its product offerings. On July 1, 2008, the cooperative transitioned into a corporation.Carefully selecting the factory location to ensure optimal water quality, reliable raw material procurement, and efficient distribution, the distillery was constructed near Lake Ikeda in Ibusuki, an area ideal for shochu production.Our main brand is Riemon, which we focus on producing. breweries.description: Since founded in 1890, this sake brewery has continued to create new values, including Japan's first barley shochu stored in casks, "music brewing" to promote fermentation and maturation, and long-term storage that spares no time for slow maturation. The brewery has been awarded an honorable mention in the Kagoshima Prefecture Honkaku shochu Competition for more than 40 consecutive years. The brewery is highly regarded for its technical capabilities. Representative brands are the barrel-aged barley shochu "Den-en Gold Label" and the barrel-aged sweet potato shochu "Den-en Enveresida," which has won numerous awards at domestic and international competitions since its release in 2018. breweries.description: More than a century ago, the first-generation head of our distillery founded it in Ichikikushikino City in search of excellent water.The spring water discovered here is praised as exceptionally pure and rare. Combined with carefully selected potatoes, this water has been the foundation of Satsuma Tanabata, our traditional potato shochu made with dedication since the distillery’s inception.We honor time-honored traditions and uncompromising craftsmanship in our shochu-making. Using rice harvested from nearby terraced fields as koji rice, we employ a unique fermentation method to mature our shochu carefully.We invite you to savor our aged potato shochu, crafted with pride by our master artisans here in Kagoshima. breweries.name: Seifuku Shuzou
breweries.address: 959, Miyara, Ishigaki Shi, Okinawa Ken, 907-0243, Japan
brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Founded in 1949 in Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture, we preserve the traditional direct fire distillation method for crafting awamori. At the same time, we were pioneers in the awamori industry, developing awamori-based liqueurs such as plum and yuzu liqueurs.In 2018, we revived Imuge, an ancient Ryukyu spirit that had disappeared for over 100 years. In 2021, we began producing rum using sugarcane from Ishigaki Island. Looking ahead, we plan to start brewing Japanese sake using Okinawan-grown rice in 2026.Our mission is to bring innovation to the awamori industry, and we continue to strive toward this goal with passion and determination. Business Hours:10:00 AM – 5:00 PMBusiness Days: Weekdays (closed on weekends and public holidays)Factory Tour:Available at 11:00 AM, 1:30 PM, and 4:00 PM (approximately 15 minutes each)Tasting:Various tastings available

brands: Japan Sake and Shochu Makers Association
breweries.description: Founded in 1928 on Miyako Island, Okinawa Prefecture, our distillery has a rich history. One of our former master distillers, Keishu Taira, was awarded the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Rays, in recognition of his contributions to and the popularity of our flagship brand, Kikunotsuyu. He laid the foundation for the "perfectly balanced flavor" that defines Kikunotsuyu today.Our distillery boasts one of the largest reserves of aged awamori (kusu) in the industry, enabling a stable supply of premium aged spirits. Our flagship aged awamori, V.I.P Gold, has surpassed 10 million bottles in cumulative sales.In 2023, we launched Kikunotsuyu akari, a new staple awamori made using reduced-pressure distillation. It has received positive reviews, even from those who typically don’t enjoy awamori. Business Hours:9:30 AM – 5:00 PMBusiness Days:Monday to Friday (excluding public holidays)Factory Tours:Generally not available; however, if you are interested, please contact us in advance.

In addition to awamori made from traditional Thai rice, we also manufacture awamori made from domestic rice made using natural farming methods without fertilizers and pesticides, which we started working on in 2006.It is an awamori made with the spirit of pioneering new possibilities. breweries.description: Uehara Shuzo, established in 1947, is the oldest distillery in Itoman City, located in the southern part of Okinawa's main island. After a period of dormancy beginning in 2015, the distillery resumed awamori production in 2021. Uehara Shuzo’s awamori is crafted through the unique blessings of Okinawa and the pioneering use of "electronic techniques," a first in the awamori industry. This method produces smooth and rich awamori that has earned high acclaim, including consecutive Okinawa Governor’s Awards. breweries.description: Founded in 1955. It is an awamori manufacturer on Ishigaki Island. It is named "Yae Izumi" in the image of the springs on the islands of Mt.Yae, and sake is brewed on a small hill overlooking the islands. We are working on sake brewing that combines tradition and innovation, such as fusing long-term barrel storage. Awamori made from rice harvested on Ishigaki Island and habu sake made with red habu that inhabits it. We are working on the development of wine, vinegar, liqueurs, and other sake brewing unique to Ishigaki Island and the development of new products that make use of fermentation technology. breweries.description: We are a sake brewery with a 200-year history, located on Kamisanno Town in Hida Takayama, Gifu Prefecture. Nestled in the "Old Town," a picturesque area steeped in the charm of a historic castle town, our brewery has been crafting sake for generations.Using pristine spring water sourced from the deep Hida mountains, part of the Japanese Alps, and high-quality sake rice, we create sake with traditional techniques that have been passed down through the centuries.In addition to sake brewing, our premises feature a restaurant and a gift shop, offering a "sake theme park" experience. We look forward to welcoming you! Business Hours:9:00 AM – 6:00 PMClosed on irregular holidaysHow to Enjoy:Purchase tokens from the exchange machine, then use the gacha machine to receive an original ochoko cup. breweries.address: 7270-1, Tokicho, Mizunami Shi, Gifu Ken, 509-6101, Japan
breweries.description: For over 300 years, since the Genroku era, we have been brewing sake in the Mizunami area of Gifu Prefecture. The concept behind our products is to create sake that enhances the dining experience. To achieve this, we intentionally keep the aroma subtle, allowing the natural flavors of the rice to shine through, while crafting a sake that is clean, smooth, and crisp on the palate.Due to our limited production volume, our sake is not widely available. If you happen to find it, we hope you seize the opportunity to enjoy it without hesitation. Business Hours:9:00 AM – 5:00 PMClosed on irregular holidays (generally on weekends and public holidays)Retail Sales:Available at the store, but tastings (paid or free) are not offered.Brewery Tours:Typically held twice a year, once in mid-February and once in early April (please check our website for details).

breweries.name: Komachi Shuzou
breweries.address: 2-15, Soharaibukicho, Kakamigahara Shi, Gifu Ken, 504-0851, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1894, our brewery crafts local sake under the Nagaragawa brand, named after the pristine waters of the Nagara River, home to the traditional cormorant fishing. Situated at the base of a mountain dedicated to Ryujin, the water deity, our brewery uses pure spring water from underground streams and contract-grown sake rice. The sake is brewed with traditional techniques that draw out the rich umami of the rice, resulting in a mild, comforting flavor with a clean finish, thanks to the soft water.We are also known as a brewery that "brews sake with music," creating a harmonious environment during production. Our sake has earned high praise, with numerous awards including consecutive gold medals at international competitions such as the IWC. Currently, we export to five countries worldwide, where our sake is highly regarded for its quality. Business Hours:9:00 AM – 5:00 PMClosed on public holidays and New Year holidaysOpen Monday to Friday

breweries.name: Watanabe Shuzouten
breweries.address: 7-7, Furukawacho Ichinomachi, Hida Shi, Gifu Ken, 509-4234, Japan
breweries.description: Using pristine groundwater from the Hida Mountain Range and premium sake rice, primarily Hidahomare, the brewery creates exceptional sake with a rich umami profile.From October to April, the brewery produces 550,000 bottles of sake, but this is the maximum capacity, making all their offerings limited editions.Their sake-making process still relies heavily on human craftsmanship. Traditional wooden tools are used, and the brewers value the direct experience of aromas and textures through their hands.Each bottle is crafted with heartfelt dedication, aiming to create a sake so memorable that customers will say, “I’m so glad I discovered Hourai!” This is the guiding spirit behind their pursuit of exquisite sake. Business Hours:9:00 AM – 4:00 PMBusiness Days:Irregular holidaysBrewery Tours:Brewery tours are scheduled to resume in the spring of 2025. breweries.name: Tokoro Shuzou
breweries.address: 537-1, Miwa, Ibigawacho, Ibi Gun, Gifu Ken, 501-0619, Japan
breweries.description: We are a small sake brewery located at the uppermost reaches of the Ibi River, one of the Kiso Three Rivers. Our goal is to craft sake that balances the rich flavors of rice with a clean, crisp finish, achieved through meticulous brewing practices.Our main brand, Bojimaya, consists entirely of junmai sake, with approximately 70% of it being unfiltered, unpasteurized, undiluted sake (muroka nama genshu). We are dedicated to creating sake that values individuality and character. Business Hours:10:00 AM – 5:00 PMClosed on Sundays, public holidays, and New Year holidays (subject to occasional closure)Brewery Tours: By Appointment

breweries.name: Harada Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 10, Kamisannomachi, Takayama Shi, Gifu Ken, 506-0846, Japan
breweries.description: With over 200 years of history, our brewery was founded in the late Edo period. Under the brand name Sansha, it produces and sells Japanese sake. Using the harsh winter climate of the Hida region to its advantage, the brewery employs the traditional Hida-style brewing during the coldest winter months to craft its sake.While preserving its time-honored traditions, the brewery also explores innovation by using flower yeast, a natural sake yeast cultured from flower nectar, to create new styles of sake.At the directly managed store, visitors can purchase the full range of its sake, along with unique souvenirs such as sake-infused cheesecakes, sake manju, and delicacies. The adjoining café corner offers treats like amazake and Hida beef buns for a delightful experience. Business Hours:April – October: 8:30 AM – 6:00 PMNovember – March: 8:30 AM – 5:00 PMOpen year-roundPaid Tasting: Available. Purchase an ochoko cup for 450 yen to sample over 10 types of sake, one tasting per type. The cup may be taken home.

breweries.name: Ena Jouzou
breweries.address: 2992-1, Fukuoka, Nakatsugawa Shi, Gifu Ken, 508-0203, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1818 (Bunsei 1) during the late Edo period, our brewery began when the fifth-generation head of the Nagase family, Kichiemon, acquired a sake brewing license while continuing his work as a farmer.The soft water used for brewing flows abundantly from Mt. Futatsumori, which towers behind the brewery. Situated in a remote mountain area at an elevation of 600 meters, the environment is ideal for sake brewing, with low temperatures and cold, clear water.Here, the 11th-generation head of the Nagase family personally serves as the brewmaster, continuing the family-run operation.While large-scale production is not possible, we are committed to crafting small batches of sake that we can personally ensure meet our standard of "deliciousness." In 2022, the sales shop underwent a major renovation.In addition to purchasing sake and free tastings, standing bar service (paid dining) is now also available.

Using spring water from Mount Ena as our brewing water, we craft fruity and aromatic sake made from rice and water, delivering both deliciousness and joy to our customers. breweries.description: Founded in 1902 (Meiji 35), our brewery has been deeply committed to sake-making for generations. Even after more than 120 years, we remain dedicated to creating flavors unique to “this land” and “this brewery,” ensuring that our sake continues to be cherished by the local community.Our fourth-generation brewmaster, Asako Watanabe, is the only female brewmaster in Gifu Prefecture. While inheriting the techniques of her predecessors, she strives to craft sake that only she can create—sake that brings joy to those who taste it. breweries.address: 134-1, Ueno, Nakatsugawa Shi, Gifu Ken, 509-9231, Japan
breweries.description: Our brewery, located in Nakatsugawa City, Gifu Prefecture, is the smallest sake brewery in Japan, with a history dating back to the Edo period.With an annual production of just 50 koku (about 9,000 liters), we are a truly small-scale brewery. We use the soft spring water of Onozawa, which flows from the mountain behind the brewery, and adhere to traditional methods. Our rice is steamed in wooden vats, and the sake is pressed using wooden tanks, ensuring that every step of the process is entirely handcrafted. breweries.description: Surrounded by mountains and blessed with abundant water, our brewery has been crafting sake in Hida-Furukawa for over 320 years. Using locally sourced rice, water, and the dedication of local people, we have continued to produce sake that reflects the refreshing climate and natural beauty of the Hida region, earning the love and support of the local community.We take pride in being a sake brewery beloved by the people of Hida and remain dedicated to crafting sake that captures the true essence of our flagship brand, Shiramayumi. breweries.name: Yamada Shouten (Kuramoto Yamada)
breweries.address: 3888-2, Yaotsu, Yaotsucho, Kamo Gun, Gifu Ken, 505-0301, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1868 (Meiji 1), our brewery has been crafting sake for generations in the picturesque town of Yaotsu, surrounded by beautiful mountains.Located in an area once known as "Tamai," named after the old "Tamanoi" well, we use water drawn from this soft-water well. The resulting sake is gentle, with a smooth and pleasant mouthfeel.With a short brewing season limited to the winter months, we produce all of our sake by hand. Our ideal is to craft sake that is smooth, enjoyable, and easy to drink—sake that stands apart from conventional notions of sweet or dry, light or full-bodied, but is instead a companion for the long term. Brewery tours and tastings inside the facility are not available.

breweries.name: Hirano Jouzou
breweries.address: 164, Yamatocho Tokunaga, Gujo Shi, Gifu Ken, 501-4607, Japan
breweries.description: Founded in 1873 (Meiji 6), Hirano Jozo, the maker of "Bojo," is located in Yamato Town, Gujo City, Gifu Prefecture.This town, known as the "Land of Kokin-Denju," is celebrated as a historical hub of Japanese poetry. Particularly famous is the "Water of Kokin-Denju no Sato," renowned for its quality as one of Japan's finest waters. At Hirano Jozo, this water is drawn into the brewery’s garden and used not only for brewing sake but also for washing rice and cleaning tools. The saying "Great sake comes from great water" has long been known, and this exceptional water is undoubtedly the secret behind the delicious taste of the Oku Mino sake brand, "Bojo." Business Hours: 8:00 AM to 5:00 PMClosed on: Saturdays, Sundays, Golden Week, Obon holidays, New Year holidays, and other occasions.Tours and Tastings: Available by reservation only.Note: During the brewing season (November to around March), tastings and sales are available, but tours are not permitted.

breweries.name: Kawashiri Shuzoujou
breweries.address: 68, Kamininomachi, Takayama Shi, Gifu Ken, 506-0845, Japan
breweries.description: Hida Takayama, surrounded by majestic mountains such as Norikura, Hakusan, and Ontake, is a sacred land for sake brewing, blessed with cold winter climates, pristine brewing water, and excellent sake rice.Our brewery was founded in 1839 (Tenpo 10) during the late Edo period. In the 1970s, we shifted our focus entirely to quality, eliminating the use of additives such as sugar and flavoring agents, as well as discontinuing OEM production for major brands. Since then, we have specialized in direct sales and production.Our sake is known for its rich and full-bodied flavor. When newly brewed, it can be rough in taste and requires long-term aging to achieve a smooth and mellow character.As a local sake producer, we exclusively use rice grown in Hida and specialize in aged sake (koshu), focusing on producing mature, well-aged sake after several years of storage.
